|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- Run the Chain \-\--]{.c32}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [async def run_parallel_example(topic: str) -\> None:]{.c32}          |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"]{.c32}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   Asynchronously invokes the parallel processing chain with a       |
| specific topic]{.c32}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [   and prints the synthesized result.]{.c32}                         |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   Args:]{.c32}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       topic: The input topic to be processed by the LangChain       |
| chains.]{.c32}                                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"]{.c32}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   if not llm:]{.c32}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [       print(\"LLM not initialized. Cannot run example.\")]{.c32}    |
|                                                                       |
| [       return]{.c32}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   print(f\"\\n\-\-- Running Parallel LangChain Example for Topic:   |
| \'{topic}\' \-\--\")]{.c32}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   try:]{.c32}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [       # The input to \`ainvoke\` is the single \'topic\' string,    |
| ]{.c32}                                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [       # then passed to each runnable in the \`map_chain\`.]{.c32}   |
|                                                                       |
| [       response = await full_parallel_chain.ainvoke(topic)]{.c32}    |
|                                                                       |
| [       print(\"\\n\-\-- Final Response \-\--\")]{.c32}               |
|                                                                       |
| [       print(response)]{.c32}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [   except Exception as e:]{.c32}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [       print(f\"\\nAn error occurred during chain execution:         |
| {e}\")]{.c32}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [if \_\_name\_\_ == \"\_\_main\_\_\":]{.c32}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   test_topic = \"The history of space exploration\"]{.c32}          |
|                                                                       |
| [   # In Python 3.7+, asyncio.run is the standard way to run an async |
| function.]{.c32}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   asyncio.run(run_parallel_example(test_topic))]{.c32}              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c9}

[The provided Python code implements a LangChain application designed
for processing a given topic efficiently by leveraging parallel
execution. Note that ]{.c10} [asyncio provides ]{.c10}
[concurrency]{.c10} [, not parallelism. It achieves this on a single
thread by using an event loop that intelligently switches between tasks
when one is idle (e.g., waiting for a network request). This creates the
effect of multiple tasks progressing at once, but the code itself is
still being executed by only one thread, constrained by Python\'s Global
Interpreter Lock (GIL).]{.c10} [ ]{.c9}

[The code begins by importing essential modules from ]{.c10}
[langchain_openai]{.c10} [ and ]{.c10} [langchain_core]{.c10} [,
including components for language models, prompts, output parsing, and
runnable structures. The code attempts to initialize a ]{.c10}
[ChatOpenAI]{.c10} [ instance, specifically using the \"gpt-4o-mini\"
model, with a specified temperature for controlling creativity. A
try-except block is used for robustness during the language model
initialization. Three independent LangChain \"chains\" are then defined,
each designed to perform a distinct task on the input topic. The first
chain is for summarizing the topic concisely, using a system message and
a user message containing the topic placeholder. The second chain is
configured to generate three interesting questions related to the topic.
The third chain is set up to identify between 5 and 10 key terms from
the input topic, requesting them to be comma-separated. Each of these
independent chains consists of a ]{.c10} [ChatPromptTemplate]{.c10}
[ tailored to its specific task, followed by the initialized language
model and a ]{.c10} [StrOutputParser]{.c10} [ to format the output as a
string. ]{.c9}

[A ]{.c10} [RunnableParallel]{.c10} [ block is then constructed to
bundle these three chains, allowing them to execute simultaneously. This
parallel runnable also includes a ]{.c10} [RunnablePassthrough]{.c10}
[ to ensure the original input topic is available for subsequent steps.
A separate ]{.c10} [ChatPromptTemplate]{.c10} [ is defined for the final
synthesis step, taking the summary, questions, key terms, and the
original topic as input to generate a comprehensive answer. The full
end-to-end processing chain, named ]{.c10} [full_parallel_chain]{.c10}
[, is created by sequencing the ]{.c10} [map_chain]{.c10} [ (the
parallel block) into the synthesis prompt, followed by the language
model and the output parser. An asynchronous function ]{.c10}
[run_parallel_example]{.c10} [ is provided to demonstrate how to invoke
this ]{.c10} [full_parallel_chain]{.c10} [. This function takes the
topic as input and uses ]{.c10} [invoke]{.c10} [ to run the asynchronous
chain. Finally, the standard Python ]{.c10} [if \_\_name\_\_ ==
\"\_\_main\_\_\":]{.c10} [ block shows how to execute the ]{.c10}
[run_parallel_example]{.c10} [ with a sample topic, in this case, \"The
history of space exploration\", using ]{.c10} [asyncio.run]{.c10} [ to
manage the asynchronous execution.]{.c10}

[In essence, this code sets up a workflow where multiple LLM calls (for
summarizing, questions, and terms) happen at the same time for a given
topic, and their results are then combined by a final LLM call. This
showcases the core idea of parallelization in an agentic workflow using
LangChain.]{.c9}

# [Hands-On Code Example (Google ADK)]{.c4} {#Chapter3Parallelization.xhtml_h.iqrupm7bq06v .c3}

[Okay, let\'s now turn our attention to a concrete example illustrating
these concepts within the Google ADK framework. We\'ll examine how the
ADK primitives, such as ParallelAgent and SequentialAgent, can be
applied to build an agent flow that leverages concurrent execution for
improved efficiency.]{.c9}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [from google.adk.agents import LlmAgent, ParallelAgent,               |
| SequentialAgent]{.c32}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.tools import google_search]{.c32}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [GEMINI_MODEL=\"gemini-2.0-flash\"]{.c32}                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- 1. Define Researcher Sub-Agents (to run in parallel)        |
| \-\--]{.c32}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Researcher 1: Renewable Energy]{.c32}                             |
|                                                                       |
| [researcher_agent_1 = LlmAgent(]{.c32}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [    name=\"RenewableEnergyResearcher\",]{.c32}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [    model=GEMINI_MODEL,]{.c32}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [    instruction=\"\"\"You are an AI Research Assistant specializing  |
| in energy.]{.c32}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [Research the latest advancements in \'renewable energy               |
| sources\'.]{.c32}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [Use the Google Search tool provided.]{.c32}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [Summarize your key findings concisely (1-2 sentences).]{.c32}        |
|                                                                       |
| [Output \*only\* the summary.]{.c32}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [\"\"\",]{.c32}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [    description=\"Researches renewable energy sources.\",]{.c32}     |
|                                                                       |
| [    tools=\[google_search\],]{.c32}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [    \# Store result in state for the merger agent]{.c32}             |
|                                                                       |
| [    output_key=\"renewable_energy_result\"]{.c32}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c32}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Researcher 2: Electric Vehicles]{.c32}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [researcher_agent_2 = LlmAgent(]{.c32}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [    name=\"EVResearcher\",]{.c32}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [    model=GEMINI_MODEL,]{.c32}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [    instruction=\"\"\"You are an AI Research Assistant specializing  |
| in transportation.]{.c32}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [Research the latest developments in \'electric vehicle               |
| technology\'.]{.c32}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [Use the Google Search tool provided.]{.c32}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [Summarize your key findings concisely (1-2 sentences).]{.c32}        |
|                                                                       |
| [Output \*only\* the summary.]{.c32}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [\"\"\",]{.c32}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [    description=\"Researches electric vehicle technology.\",]{.c32}  |
|                                                                       |
| [    tools=\[google_search\],]{.c32}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [    \# Store result in state for the merger agent]{.c32}             |
|                                                                       |
| [    output_key=\"ev_technology_result\"]{.c32}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c32}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Researcher 3: Carbon Capture]{.c32}                               |
|                                                                       |
| [researcher_agent_3 = LlmAgent(]{.c32}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [    name=\"CarbonCaptureResearcher\",]{.c32}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [    model=GEMINI_MODEL,]{.c32}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [    instruction=\"\"\"You are an AI Research Assistant specializing  |
| in climate solutions.]{.c32}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [Research the current state of \'carbon capture methods\'.]{.c32}     |
|                                                                       |
| [Use the Google Search tool provided.]{.c32}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [Summarize your key findings concisely (1-2 sentences).]{.c32}        |
|                                                                       |
| [Output \*only\* the summary.]{.c32}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [\"\"\",]{.c32}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [    description=\"Researches carbon capture methods.\",]{.c32}       |
|                                                                       |
| [    tools=\[google_search\],]{.c32}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [    \# Store result in state for the merger agent]{.c32}             |
|                                                                       |
| [    output_key=\"carbon_capture_result\"]{.c32}                      |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c32}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- 2. Create the ParallelAgent (Runs researchers concurrently) |
| \-\--]{.c32}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [\# This agent orchestrates the concurrent execution of the           |
| researchers.]{.c32}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [\# It finishes once all researchers have completed and stored their  |
| results in state.]{.c32}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [parallel_research_agent = ParallelAgent(]{.c32}                      |
|                                                                       |
| [    name=\"ParallelWebResearchAgent\",]{.c32}                        |
|                                                                       |
| [    sub_agents=\[researcher_agent_1, researcher_agent_2,             |
| researcher_agent_3\],]{.c32}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [    description=\"Runs multiple research agents in parallel to       |
| gather information.\"]{.c32}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c32}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- 3. Define the Merger Agent (Runs \*after\* the parallel     |
| agents) \-\--]{.c32}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [\# This agent takes the results stored in the session state by the   |
| parallel agents]{.c32}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [\# and synthesizes them into a single, structured response with      |
| attributions.]{.c32}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [merger_agent = LlmAgent(]{.c32}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [    name=\"SynthesisAgent\",]{.c32}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [    model=GEMINI_MODEL,  # Or potentially a more powerful model if   |
| needed for synthesis]{.c32}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [    instruction=\"\"\"You are an AI Assistant responsible for        |
| combining research findings into a structured report.]{.c32}          |
|                                                                       |
| [Your primary task is to synthesize the following research summaries, |
| clearly attributing findings to their source areas. Structure your    |
| response using headings for each topic. Ensure the report is coherent |
| and integrates the key points smoothly.]{.c32}                        |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\*\*Crucially: Your entire response MUST be grounded \*exclusively\* |
| on the information provided in the \'Input Summaries\' below. Do NOT  |
| add any external knowledge, facts, or details not present in these    |
| specific summaries.\*\*]{.c32}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\*\*Input Summaries:\*\*]{.c32}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\*   \*\*Renewable Energy:\*\*]{.c32}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [    {renewable_energy_result}]{.c32}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [\*   \*\*Electric Vehicles:\*\*]{.c32}                               |
|                                                                       |
| [    {ev_technology_result}]{.c32}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [\*   \*\*Carbon Capture:\*\*]{.c32}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [    {carbon_capture_result}]{.c32}                                   |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\*\*Output Format:\*\*]{.c32}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\## Summary of Recent Sustainable Technology Advancements]{.c32}     |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\### Renewable Energy Findings]{.c32}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [(Based on RenewableEnergyResearcher\'s findings)]{.c32}              |
|                                                                       |
| [\[Synthesize and elaborate \*only\* on the renewable energy input    |
| summary provided above.\]]{.c32}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\### Electric Vehicle Findings]{.c32}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [(Based on EVResearcher\'s findings)]{.c32}                           |
|                                                                       |
| [\[Synthesize and elaborate \*only\* on the EV input summary provided |
| above.\]]{.c32}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\### Carbon Capture Findings]{.c32}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [(Based on CarbonCaptureResearcher\'s findings)]{.c32}                |
|                                                                       |
| [\[Synthesize and elaborate \*only\* on the carbon capture input      |
| summary provided above.\]]{.c32}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\### Overall Conclusion]{.c32}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [\[Provide a brief (1-2 sentence) concluding statement that connects  |
| \*only\* the findings presented above.\]]{.c32}                       |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [Output \*only\* the structured report following this format. Do not  |
| include introductory or concluding phrases outside this structure,    |
| and strictly adhere to using only the provided input summary          |
| content.]{.c32}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [\"\"\",]{.c32}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [    description=\"Combines research findings from parallel agents    |
| into a structured, cited report, strictly grounded on provided        |
| inputs.\",]{.c32}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [    \# No tools needed for merging]{.c32}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [    \# No output_key needed here, as its direct response is the      |
| final output of the sequence]{.c32}                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c32}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c32}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- 4. Create the SequentialAgent (Orchestrates the overall     |
| flow) \-\--]{.c32}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [\# This is the main agent that will be run. It first executes the    |
| ParallelAgent]{.c32}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [\# to populate the state, and then executes the MergerAgent to       |
| produce the final output.]{.c32}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [sequential_pipeline_agent = SequentialAgent(]{.c32}                  |
|                                                                       |
| [    name=\"ResearchAndSynthesisPipeline\",]{.c32}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [    \# Run parallel research first, then merge]{.c32}                |
|                                                                       |
| [    sub_agents=\[parallel_research_agent, merger_agent\],]{.c32}     |
|                                                                       |
| [    description=\"Coordinates parallel research and synthesizes the  |
| results.\"]{.c32}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c32}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [root_agent = sequential_pipeline_agent]{.c32}                        |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c37}

[This code defines a multi-agent system used to research and synthesize
information on sustainable technology advancements. It sets up three
LlmAgent instances to act as specialized researchers. ResearcherAgent_1
focuses on renewable energy sources, ResearcherAgent_2 researches
electric vehicle technology, and ResearcherAgent_3 investigates carbon
capture methods. Each researcher agent is configured to use a
GEMINI_MODEL and the google_search tool. They are instructed to
summarize their findings concisely (1-2 sentences) and store these
summaries in the session state using output_key.]{.c9}

[A ParallelAgent named ParallelWebResearchAgent is then created to run
these three researcher agents concurrently. This allows the research to
be conducted in parallel, potentially saving time. The ParallelAgent
completes its execution once all its sub-agents (the researchers) have
finished and populated the state.]{.c9}

[Next, a MergerAgent (also an LlmAgent) is defined to synthesize the
research results. This agent takes the summaries stored in the session
state by the parallel researchers as input. Its instruction emphasizes
that the output must be strictly based only on the provided input
summaries, prohibiting the addition of external knowledge. The
MergerAgent is designed to structure the combined findings into a report
with headings for each topic and a brief overall conclusion.]{.c9}

[Finally, a SequentialAgent named ResearchAndSynthesisPipeline is
created to orchestrate the entire workflow. As the primary controller,
t]{.c10} [his main agent first executes t]{.c10} [he ParallelAgent to
perform the research. Once the ParallelAgent is complete, the
SequentialAgent then executes the MergerAgent to synthesize the
collected information. The sequential_pipeline_agent is set as the
root_agent, representing the entry point for running this multi-agent
system. The overall process is designed to efficiently gather
information from multiple sources in parallel and then combine it into a
single, structured report.]{.c9}

# [At a Glance]{.c4} {#Chapter3Parallelization.xhtml_h.w88iw8je1xab .c38}

[What: ]{.c39} [Many agentic workflows involve multiple sub-tasks that
must be completed to achieve a final goal. A purely sequential
execution, where each task waits for the previous one to finish, is
often inefficient and slow. This latency becomes a significant
bottleneck when tasks depend on external I/O operations, such as calling
different APIs or querying multiple databases. Without a mechanism for
concurrent execution, the total processing time is the sum of all
individual task durations, hindering the system\'s overall performance
and responsiveness.]{.c9}

[Why: ]{.c39} [The Parallelization pattern provides a standardized
solution by enabling the simultaneous execution of independent tasks. It
works by identifying components of a workflow, like tool usages or LLM
calls, that do not rely on each other\'s immediate outputs. Agentic
frameworks like LangChain and the Google ADK provide built-in constructs
to define and manage these concurrent operations. For instance, a main
process can invoke several sub-tasks that run in parallel and wait for
all of them to complete before proceeding to the next step. By running
these independent tasks at the same time rather than one after another,
this pattern drastically reduces the total execution time.]{.c9}

[Rule of thumb: ]{.c39} [Use this pattern when a workflow contains
multiple independent operations that can run simultaneously, such as
fetching data from several APIs, processing different chunks of data, or
generating multiple pieces of content for later synthesis.]{.c9}

[Visual summary]{.c40}

[ ![](9/GoogleDoc/images/image1.jpg){.calibre1} ]{.calibre}

[Fig.2: Parallelization design pattern]{.c9}

[]{.c9}

# [Key ]{.calibre2} [Takeaways]{.c4} {#Chapter3Parallelization.xhtml_h.fw5qx3kv4vi8 .c3}

[Here are the key takeaways:]{.c2}

- [Parallelization]{.c10} [ is a pattern for executing independent tasks
  concurrently to improve efficiency.]{.c9}
- [It is particularly useful when tasks involve waiting for external
  resources, such as API calls.]{.c9}
- [The adoption of a concurrent or parallel architecture introduces
  substantial complexity and cost, impacting key development phases such
  as design, debugging, and system logging.]{.c9}
- [Frameworks like LangChain and Google ADK provide built-in support for
  defining and managing parallel execution.]{.c9}
- [In LangChain Expression Language (LCEL), RunnableParallel is a key
  construct for running multiple runnables side-by-side.]{.c9}
- [Google ADK can facilitate parallel execution through ]{.c10}
  [LLM-Driven Delegation]{.c10} [, where a Coordinator agent\'s LLM
  identifies independent sub-tasks and triggers their concurrent
  handling by specialized sub-agents.]{.c9}
- [Parallelization helps reduce overall latency and makes agentic
  systems more responsive for complex tasks.]{.c9}

# [Conclusion]{.c44} {#Chapter3Parallelization.xhtml_h.nciyzcmwqehe .c3}

[The parallelization pattern is a method for optimizing computational
workflows by concurrently executing independent sub-tasks. This approach
reduces overall latency, particularly in complex operations that involve
multiple model inferences or calls to external services.]{.c9}

[Frameworks provide distinct mechanisms for implementing this pattern.
In LangChain, constructs like RunnableParallel are used to explicitly
define and execute multiple processing chains simultaneously. In
contrast, frameworks like the Google Agent Developer Kit (ADK) can
achieve parallelization through multi-agent delegation, where a primary
coordinator model assigns different sub-tasks to specialized agents that
can operate concurrently.]{.c9}

[By integrating parallel processing with sequential (chaining) and
conditional (routing) control flows, it becomes possible to construct
sophisticated, high-performance computational systems capable of
efficiently managing diverse and complex tasks.]{.c10}

# [References]{.c44} {#Chapter3Parallelization.xhtml_h.gn4yw75k0tr6 .c3}

[Here are some resources for further reading on the Parallelization
pattern and related concepts:]{.c9}

1.  [LangChain Expression Language (LCEL) Documentation (Parallelism):
    ]{.c10} [
    [https://python.langchain.com/docs/concepts/lcel/](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://python.langchain.com/docs/concepts/lcel/&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228348345300&usg=AOvVaw0aLCq_ajmMAOoGInGWLEd6){.c48}
    ]{.c47} [ ]{.c9}
2.  [Google Agent Developer Kit (ADK) Documentation (Multi-Agent
    Systems): ]{.c10} [
    [https://google.github.io/adk-docs/agents/multi-agents/](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://google.github.io/adk-docs/agents/multi-agents/&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228348345627&usg=AOvVaw3j2fIrV1nvqdQKlV5gNqJL){.c48}
    ]{.c47}
3.  [Python asynci]{.c10} [o]{.c49} [ Documentation: ]{.c10} [
    [https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio.html](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio.html&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228348345853&usg=AOvVaw1WB3a3SuE-qrq4bOQahpir){.c48}
    ]{.c47}

[]{.c9}

[]{#Chapter4Reflection.xhtml}

[Chapter 4: ]{.calibre} [Reflection]{.calibre}

# [Reflection ]{.calibre} [Pattern Overview]{.c3} {#Chapter4Reflection.xhtml_h.2qupshqhpi3f .c2}

[In the preceding chapters, we\'ve explored fundamental agentic
patterns: Chaining for sequential execution, Routing for dynamic path
selection, and Parallelization for concurrent task execution. These
patterns enable agents to perform complex tasks more efficiently and
flexibly. However, even with sophisticated workflows, an agent\'s
initial output or plan might not be optimal, accurate, or complete. This
is where the ]{.c5} [Reflection]{.c6} [ pattern comes into play.]{.c7}

[The Reflection pattern involves an agent evaluating its own work,
output, or internal state and using that evaluation to improve its
performance or refine its response. It\'s a form of self-correction or
self-improvement, allowing the agent to iteratively refine its output or
adjust its approach based on feedback, internal critique, or comparison
against desired criteria. ]{.c5} [Reflection can occasionally be
facilitated by a separate agent whose specific role is to analyze the
output of an initial agent.]{.c7}

[Unlike a simple sequential chain where output is passed directly to the
next step, or routing which chooses a path, reflection introduces a
feedback loop. The agent doesn\'t just produce an output; it then
examines that output (or the process that generated it), identifies
potential issues or areas for improvement, and uses those insights to
generate a better version or modify its future actions.]{.c7}

[The process typically involves:]{.c7}

1.  [Execution:]{.c6} [ The agent performs a task or generates an
    initial output.]{.c7}
2.  [Evaluation/Critique:]{.c6} [ The agent (often using another LLM
    call or a set of rules) analyzes the result from the previous step.
    This evaluation might check for factual accuracy, coherence, style,
    completeness, adherence to instructions, or other relevant
    criteria.]{.c7}
3.  [Reflection/Refinement:]{.c6} [ Based on the critique, the agent
    determines how to improve. This might involve generating a refined
    output, adjusting parameters for a subsequent step, or even
    modifying the overall plan.]{.c7}
4.  [Iteration (Optional but common):]{.c6} [ The refined output or
    adjusted approach can then be executed, and the reflection process
    can repeat until a satisfactory result is achieved or a stopping
    condition is met.]{.c7}

[]{.c7}

[A key and highly effective implementation of the Reflection pattern
separates the process into two distinct logical roles: a Producer and a
Critic. This is often called the \"Generator-Critic\" or
\"Producer-Reviewer\" model. While a single agent can perform
self-reflection, using two specialized agents (or two separate LLM calls
with distinct system prompts) often yields more robust and unbiased
results.]{.c7}

[1. The Producer Agent: This agent\'s primary responsibility is to
perform the initial execution of the task. It focuses entirely on
generating the content, whether it\'s writing code, drafting a blog
post, or creating a plan. It takes the initial prompt and produces the
first version of the output.]{.c7}

[2. The Critic Agent: This agent\'s sole purpose is to evaluate the
output generated by the Producer. It is given a different set of
instructions, often a distinct persona (e.g., \"You are a senior
software engineer,\" \"You are a meticulous fact-checker\"). The
Critic\'s instructions guide it to analyze the Producer\'s work against
specific criteria, such as factual accuracy, code quality, stylistic
requirements, or completeness. It is designed to find flaws, suggest
improvements, and provide structured feedback.]{.c7}

[This separation of concerns is powerful because it prevents the
\"cognitive bias\" of an agent reviewing its own work. The Critic agent
approaches the output with a fresh perspective, dedicated entirely to
finding errors and areas for improvement. The feedback from the Critic
is then passed back to the Producer agent, which uses it as a guide to
generate a new, refined version of the output. The provided LangChain
and ADK code examples both implement this two-agent model: the LangChain
example uses a specific \"reflector_prompt\" to create a critic persona,
while the ADK example explicitly defines a producer and a reviewer
agent.]{.c14}

[Implementing reflection often requires structuring the agent\'s
workflow to include these feedback loops. This can be achieved through
iterative loops in code, or using frameworks that support state
management and conditional transitions based on evaluation results.
While a single step of evaluation and refinement can be implemented
]{.c5} [within either a ]{.c14} [LangChain/Lang]{.c5} [Graph, or ADK, or
Crew.AI]{.c14} [ chain, true iterative reflection typically involves
more complex orchestration.]{.c7}

[The Reflection pattern is crucial for building agents that can produce
high-quality outputs, handle nuanced tasks, and exhibit a degree of
self-awareness and adaptability. It moves agents beyond simply executing
instructions towards a more sophisticated form of problem-solving and
content generation.]{.c7}

[The intersection of reflection with goal setting and monitoring (see
Chapter 11) is worth noticing. A goal provides the ultimate benchmark
for the agent\'s self-evaluation, while monitoring tracks its progress.
In a number of practical cases, Reflection then might act as the
corrective engine, using monitored feedback to analyze deviations and
adjust its strategy. This synergy transforms the agent from a passive
executor into a purposeful system that adaptively works to achieve its
objectives. ]{.c7}

[Furthermore, the effectiveness of the Reflection pattern is
significantly enhanced when the LLM keeps a memory of the conversation
]{.c14} [(see Chapter ]{.c14} [8). This conversational history provides
crucial context for the evaluation phase, allowing the agent to assess
its output not just in isolation, but against the backdrop of previous
interactions, user feedback, and evolving goals. It enables the agent to
learn from past critiques and avoid repeating errors. Without memory,
each reflection is a self-contained event; with memory, reflection
becomes a cumulative process where each cycle builds upon the last,
leading to more intelligent and context-aware refinement.]{.c7}

# [Practical Applications & Use Cases]{.c3} {#Chapter4Reflection.xhtml_h.9585w1jjz69x .c2}

[The Reflection pattern is valuable in scenarios where output quality,
accuracy, or adherence to complex constraints is critical:]{.c7}

[1. Creative Writing and Content Generation:]{.c17}

[Refining generated text, stories, poems, or marketing copy.]{.c17}

- [Use Case:]{.c6} [ An agent writing a blog post.]{.c7}

<!-- -->

- [Reflection:]{.c6} [ Generate a draft, critique it for flow, tone, and
  clarity, then rewrite based on the critique. Repeat until the post
  meets quality standards.]{.c7}
- [Benefit:]{.c6} [ Produces more polished and effective content.]{.c7}

[2. Code Generation and Debugging:]{.c17}

[Writing code, identifying errors, and fixing them.]{.c17}

- [Use Case:]{.c6} [ An agent writing a Python function.]{.c7}

<!-- -->

- [Reflection:]{.c6} [ Write initial code, run tests or static analysis,
  identify errors or inefficiencies, then modify the code based on the
  findings.]{.c7}
- [Benefit:]{.c6} [ Generates more robust and functional code.]{.c7}

[3. Complex Problem Solving:]{.c17}

[Evaluating intermediate steps or proposed solutions in multi-step
reasoning tasks.]{.c17}

- [Use Case:]{.c6} [ An agent solving a logic puzzle.]{.c7}

<!-- -->

- [Reflection:]{.c6} [ Propose a step, evaluate if it leads closer to
  the solution or introduces contradictions, backtrack or choose a
  different step if needed.]{.c7}
- [Benefit:]{.c6} [ Improves the agent\'s ability to navigate complex
  problem spaces.]{.c7}

[4. Summarization and Information Synthesis:]{.c17}

[Refining summaries for accuracy, completeness, and conciseness.]{.c17}

- [Use Case:]{.c6} [ An agent summarizing a long document.]{.c7}

<!-- -->

- [Reflection:]{.c6} [ Generate an initial summary, compare it against
  key points in the original document, refine the summary to include
  missing information or improve accuracy.]{.c7}
- [Benefit:]{.c6} [ Creates more accurate and comprehensive
  summaries.]{.c7}

[5. Planning and Strategy:]{.c17}

[Evaluating a proposed plan and identifying potential flaws or
improvements.]{.c17}

- [Use Case:]{.c6} [ An agent planning a series of actions to achieve a
  goal.]{.c7}

<!-- -->

- [Reflection:]{.c6} [ Generate a plan, simulate its execution or
  evaluate its feasibility against constraints, revise the plan based on
  the evaluation.]{.c7}
- [Benefit:]{.c6} [ Develops more effective and realistic plans.]{.c7}

[6. Conversational Agents:]{.c17}

[Reviewing previous turns in a conversation to maintain context, correct
misunderstandings, or improve response quality.]{.c17}

- [Use Case:]{.c6} [ A customer support chatbot.]{.c7}

<!-- -->

- [Reflection:]{.c6} [ After a user response, review the conversation
  history and the last generated message to ensure coherence and address
  the user\'s latest input accurately.]{.c7}
- [Benefit:]{.c6} [ Leads to more natural and effective
  conversations.]{.c7}

[Reflection adds a layer of meta-cognition to agentic systems, enabling
them to learn from their own outputs and processes, leading to more
intelligent, reliable, and high-quality results.]{.c7}

# [Hands-On Code Example (LangChain)]{.calibre} {#Chapter4Reflection.xhtml_h.t4inrafdl3sf .c2}

[The implementation of a complete, iterative reflection process
necessitates mechanisms for state management and cyclical execution.
While these are handled natively in graph-based frameworks like
LangGraph or through custom procedural code, the fundamental principle
of a single reflection cycle can be demonstrated effectively using the
compositional syntax of ]{.c14} [LCEL]{.c14} [ ]{.c14} [(LangChain
Expression Language).]{.c7}

[This ]{.c14} [example ]{.c14} [implements a reflection loop using the
Langchain library and OpenAI\'s GPT-4o model to iteratively generate and
refine a Python function that calculates the factorial of a number. The
process starts with a task prompt, generates initial code, and then
repeatedly reflects on the code based on critiques from a simulated
senior software engineer role, refining the code in each iteration until
the critique stage determines the code is perfect or a maximum number of
iterations is reached. Finally, it prints the resulting refined
code.]{.c7}

[First, ensure you have the necessary libraries installed:]{.c5}

  --------------------------------------------------------------------
  [pip install langchain langchain-community langchain-openai]{.c27}
  --------------------------------------------------------------------

[]{.c7}

[You will also need to set up your environment with your API key for the
language model you choose (e.g., OpenAI, Google Gemini,
Anthropic).]{.c5}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [import os]{.c27}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [from dotenv import load_dotenv]{.c27}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI]{.c27}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate]{.c27}         |
|                                                                       |
| [from langchain_core.messages import SystemMessage,                   |
| HumanMessage]{.c27}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c27}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- Configuration \-\--]{.c27}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Load environment variables from .env file (for                    |
| OPENAI_API_KEY)]{.c27}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [load_dotenv()]{.c27}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c27}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Check if the API key is set]{.c27}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [if not os.getenv(\"OPENAI_API_KEY\"):]{.c27}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   raise ValueError(\"OPENAI_API_KEY not found in .env file. Please  |
| add it.\")]{.c27}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c27}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Initialize the Chat LLM. We use gpt-4o for better                 |
| reasoning.]{.c27}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [\# A lower temperature is used for more deterministic                |
| outputs.]{.c27}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [llm = ChatOpenAI(model=\"gpt-4o\", temperature=0.1)]{.c27}           |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c27}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [def run_reflection_loop():]{.c27}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"]{.c27}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   Demonstrates a multi-step AI reflection loop to progressively     |
| improve a Python function.]{.c27}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"]{.c27}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   # \-\-- The Core Task \-\--]{.c27}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [   task_prompt = \"\"\"]{.c27}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   Your task is to create a Python function named                    |
| \`calculate_factorial\`.]{.c27}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   This function should do the following:]{.c27}                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   1.  Accept a single integer \`n\` as input.]{.c27}                |
|                                                                       |
| [   2.  Calculate its factorial (n!).]{.c27}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   3.  Include a clear docstring explaining what the function        |
| does.]{.c27}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   4.  Handle edge cases: The factorial of 0 is 1.]{.c27}            |
|                                                                       |
| [   5.  Handle invalid input: Raise a ValueError if the input is a    |
| negative number.]{.c27}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"]{.c27}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   # \-\-- The Reflection Loop \-\--]{.c27}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   max_iterations = 3]{.c27}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   current_code = \"\"]{.c27}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [   # We will build a conversation history to provide context in each |
| step.]{.c27}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   message_history = \[HumanMessage(content=task_prompt)\]]{.c27}    |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c27}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c27}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   for i in range(max_iterations):]{.c27}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [       print(\"\\n\" + \"=\"\*25 + f\" REFLECTION LOOP: ITERATION    |
| {i + 1} \" + \"=\"\*25)]{.c27}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c27}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       # \-\-- 1. GENERATE / REFINE STAGE \-\--]{.c27}               |
|                                                                       |
| [       # In the first iteration, it generates. In subsequent         |
| iterations, it refines.]{.c27}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [       if i == 0:]{.c27}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [           print(\"\\n\>\>\> STAGE 1: GENERATING initial             |
| code\...\")]{.c27}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [           # The first message is just the task prompt.]{.c27}       |
|                                                                       |
| [           response = llm.invoke(message_history)]{.c27}             |
|                                                                       |
| [           current_code = response.content]{.c27}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [       else:]{.c27}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [           print(\"\\n\>\>\> STAGE 1: REFINING code based on         |
| previous critique\...\")]{.c27}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [           # The message history now contains the task, ]{.c27}      |
|                                                                       |
| [           # the last code, and the last critique.]{.c27}            |
|                                                                       |
| [           # We instruct the model to apply the critiques.]{.c27}    |
|                                                                       |
| [           message_history.append(HumanMessage(content=\"Please      |
| refine the code using the critiques provided.\"))]{.c27}              |
|                                                                       |
| [           response = llm.invoke(message_history)]{.c27}             |
|                                                                       |
| [           current_code = response.content]{.c27}                    |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c27}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       print(\"\\n\-\-- Generated Code (v\" + str(i + 1) + \")       |
| \-\--\\n\" + current_code)]{.c27}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [       message_history.append(response) \# Add the generated code to |
| history]{.c27}                                                        |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c27}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       # \-\-- 2. REFLECT STAGE \-\--]{.c27}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [       print(\"\\n\>\>\> STAGE 2: REFLECTING on the generated        |
| code\...\")]{.c27}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c27}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       # Create a specific prompt for the reflector agent.]{.c27}    |
|                                                                       |
| [       # This asks the model to act as a senior code                 |
| reviewer.]{.c27}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       reflector_prompt = \[]{.c27}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [           SystemMessage(content=\"\"\"]{.c27}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [               You are a senior software engineer and an expert      |
| ]{.c27}                                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [               in Python.]{.c27}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [               Your role is to perform a meticulous code             |
| review.]{.c27}                                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [               Critically evaluate the provided Python code based    |
| ]{.c27}                                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [               on the original task requirements.]{.c27}             |
|                                                                       |
| [               Look for bugs, style issues, missing edge cases,      |
| ]{.c27}                                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [               and areas for improvement.]{.c27}                     |
|                                                                       |
| [               If the code is perfect and meets all                  |
| requirements,]{.c27}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [               respond with the single phrase                        |
| \'CODE_IS_PERFECT\'.]{.c27}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [               Otherwise, provide a bulleted list of your            |
| critiques.]{.c27}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [           \"\"\"),]{.c27}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [           HumanMessage(content=f\"Original                          |
| Task:\\n{task_prompt}\\n\\nCode to Review:\\n{current_code}\")]{.c27} |
|                                                                       |
| [       \]]{.c27}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c27}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       critique_response = llm.invoke(reflector_prompt)]{.c27}       |
|                                                                       |
| [       critique = critique_response.content]{.c27}                   |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c27}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       # \-\-- 3. STOPPING CONDITION \-\--]{.c27}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [       if \"CODE_IS_PERFECT\" in critique:]{.c27}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [           print(\"\\n\-\-- Critique \-\--\\nNo further critiques    |
| found. The code is satisfactory.\")]{.c27}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [           break]{.c27}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c27}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       print(\"\\n\-\-- Critique \-\--\\n\" + critique)]{.c27}       |
|                                                                       |
| [       # Add the critique to the history for the next refinement     |
| loop.]{.c27}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [       message_history.append(HumanMessage(content=f\"Critique of    |
| the previous code:\\n{critique}\"))]{.c27}                            |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c27}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   print(\"\\n\" + \"=\"\*30 + \" FINAL RESULT \" +                  |
| \"=\"\*30)]{.c27}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   print(\"\\nFinal refined code after the reflection                |
| process:\\n\")]{.c27}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [   print(current_code)]{.c27}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c27}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [if \_\_name\_\_ == \"\_\_main\_\_\":]{.c27}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   run_reflection_loop()]{.c30}                                      |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c32}

[The code  begins by setting up the environment, loading API keys, and
initializing a powerful language model like GPT-4o with a low
temperature for focused outputs. The core task is defined by a prompt
asking for a Python function to calculate the factorial of a number,
including specific requirements for docstrings, edge cases (factorial of
0), and error handling for negative input. The ]{.c33}
[run_reflection_loop]{.c33} [ function orchestrates the iterative
refinement process. Within the loop, in the first iteration, the
language model generates initial code based on the task prompt. In
subsequent iterations, it refines the code based on critiques from the
previous step. A separate \"reflector\" role, also played by the
language model but with a different system prompt, acts as a senior
software engineer to critique the generated code against the original
task requirements. This critique is provided as a bulleted list of
issues or the phrase \'CODE_IS_PERFECT\' if no issues are found. The
loop continues until the critique indicates the code is perfect or a
maximum number of iterations is reached. The conversation history is
maintained and passed to the language model in each step to provide
context for both generation/refinement and reflection stages. Finally,
the script prints the last generated code version after the loop
concludes.]{.c33}

# [Hands-On Code Example (ADK)]{.calibre} {#Chapter4Reflection.xhtml_h.a7nkedxjnyap .c34}

[Let\'s now look at a conceptual code example implemented using the
Google ADK.  Specifically, the code showcases this by employing]{.c33}
[ a Generator-Critic structure, w]{.c33} [here one component (the
Generator) produces an initial result or plan, and another component
(the Critic) provides critical feedback or a critique, guiding the
Generator towards a more refined or accurate final output.]{.c17}

[]{.c17}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [from google.adk.agents import SequentialAgent, LlmAgent]{.c35}       |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c35}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# The first agent generates the initial draft.]{.c35}               |
|                                                                       |
| [generator = LlmAgent(]{.c35}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"DraftWriter\",]{.c35}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   description=\"Generates initial draft content on a given          |
| subject.\",]{.c35}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [   instruction=\"Write a short, informative paragraph about the      |
| user\'s subject.\",]{.c35}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   output_key=\"draft_text\" \# The output is saved to this state    |
| key.]{.c35}                                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c35}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c35}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# The second agent critiques the draft from the first agent.]{.c35} |
|                                                                       |
| [reviewer = LlmAgent(]{.c35}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"FactChecker\",]{.c35}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   description=\"Reviews a given text for factual accuracy and       |
| provides a structured critique.\",]{.c35}                             |
|                                                                       |
| [   instruction=\"\"\"]{.c35}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   You are a meticulous fact-checker.]{.c35}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   1. Read the text provided in the state key \'draft_text\'.]{.c35} |
|                                                                       |
| [   2. Carefully verify the factual accuracy of all claims.]{.c35}    |
|                                                                       |
| [   3. Your final output must be a dictionary containing two          |
| keys:]{.c35}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [      - \"status\": A string, either \"ACCURATE\" or                 |
| \"INACCURATE\".]{.c35}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [      - \"reasoning\": A string providing a clear explanation for    |
| your status, citing specific issues if any are found.]{.c35}          |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\",]{.c35}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [   output_key=\"review_output\" \# The structured dictionary is      |
| saved here.]{.c35}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c35}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c35}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# The SequentialAgent ensures the generator runs before the         |
| reviewer.]{.c35}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [review_pipeline = SequentialAgent(]{.c35}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"WriteAndReview_Pipeline\",]{.c35}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   sub_agents=\[generator, reviewer\]]{.c35}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c35}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c35}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Execution Flow:]{.c35}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [\# 1. generator runs -\> saves its paragraph to                      |
| state\[\'draft_text\'\].]{.c35}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [\# 2. reviewer runs -\> reads state\[\'draft_text\'\] and saves its  |
| dictionary output to state\[\'review_output\'\].]{.c35}               |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c17}

[This code demonstrates the use of a sequential agent pipeline in Google
ADK  for generating and reviewing text. It defines two LlmAgent
instances: generator and reviewer. The generator agent is designed to
create an initial draft paragraph on a given subject. It is instructed
to write a short and informative piece and saves its output to the state
key draft_text. The reviewer agent acts as a fact-checker for the text
produced by the generator. It is instructed to read the text from
draft_text and verify its factual accuracy. The reviewer\'s output is a
structured dictionary with two keys: status and reasoning. status
indicates if the text is \"ACCURATE\" or \"INACCURATE\", while reasoning
provides an explanation for the status. This dictionary is saved to the
state key review_output. A SequentialAgent named review_pipeline is
created to manage the execution order of the two agents. It ensures that
the generator runs first, followed by the reviewer. The overall
execution flow is that the generator produces text, which is then saved
to the state. Subsequently, the reviewer reads this text from the state,
performs its fact-checking, and saves its findings (the status and
reasoning) back to the state. This pipeline allows for a structured
process of content creation and review using separate agents.]{.c33}
[Note:]{.c36} [ An alternative implementation utilizing ADK\'s LoopAgent
is also available for those interested.]{.c37}

[]{.c17}

[Before concluding, it\'s important to consider that while the
Reflection pattern significantly enhances output quality, it comes with
important trade-offs. The iterative process, though powerful, can lead
to higher costs and latency, since every refinement loop may require a
new LLM call, making it suboptimal for time-sensitive applications.
Furthermore, the pattern is memory-intensive; with each iteration, the
conversational history expands, including the initial output, critique,
and subsequent refinements.]{.c33}

# [At Glance]{.c3} {#Chapter4Reflection.xhtml_h.1sgh7wybtre7 .c38}

[What:]{.c39} [ An agent\'s initial output is often suboptimal,
suffering from inaccuracies, incompleteness, or a failure to meet
complex requirements. Basic agentic workflows lack a built-in process
for the agent to recognize and fix its own errors. This is solved by
having the agent evaluate its own work or, more robustly, by introducing
a separate logical agent to act as a critic, preventing the initial
response from being the final one regardless of quality.]{.c37}

[Why:]{.c39} [ The Reflection pattern offers a solution by introducing a
mechanism for self-correction and refinement. It establishes a feedback
loop where a \"producer\" agent generates an output, and then a
\"critic\" agent (or the producer itself) evaluates it against
predefined criteria. This critique is then used to generate an improved
version. This iterative process of generation, evaluation, and
refinement progressively enhances the quality of the final result,
leading to more accurate, coherent, and reliable outcomes.]{.c37}

[Rule of thumb:]{.c39} [ Use the Reflection pattern when the quality,
accuracy, and detail of the final output are more important than speed
and cost. It is particularly effective for tasks like generating
polished long-form content, writing and debugging code, and creating
detailed plans. Employ a separate critic agent when tasks require high
objectivity or specialized evaluation that a generalist producer agent
might miss.]{.c37}

[Visual summary]{.c36}

[ ![](10/GoogleDoc/images/image1.png){.calibre2} ]{.calibre1}

[Fig. 1: Reflection design pattern, self-reflection]{.calibre}

[ ![](10/GoogleDoc/images/image2.png){.calibre4} ]{.calibre3}

[Fig.2: Reflection design pattern, producer and critique agent]{.c37}

[]{.c37}

# [Key ]{.calibre} [Takeaways]{.calibre} {#Chapter4Reflection.xhtml_h.46rdogg90zn6 .c42}

- [The primary advantage of the Reflection pattern is its ability to
  iteratively self-correct and refine outputs, leading to significantly
  higher quality, accuracy, and adherence to complex instructions.]{.c7}
- [It involves a feedback loop of execution, evaluation/critique, and
  refinement. Reflection is essential for tasks requiring high-quality,
  accurate, or nuanced outputs.]{.c7}
- [A powerful implementation is the Producer-Critic model, where a
  separate agent (or prompted role) evaluates the initial output. This
  separation of concerns enhances objectivity and allows for more
  specialized, structured feedback.]{.c7}
- [However, these benefits come at the cost of increased latency and
  computational expense, along with a higher risk of exceeding the
  model\'s context window or being throttled by API services.]{.c7}
- [While full iterative reflection often requires stateful workflows
  (like LangGraph), a single reflection step can be implemented in
  LangChain using LCEL to pass output for critique and subsequent
  refinement.]{.c7}
- [Google ADK can facilitate reflection through sequential workflows
  where one agent\'s output is critiqued by another agent, allowing for
  subsequent refinement steps.]{.c7}
- [This pattern enables agents to perform self-correction and enhance
  their performance over time.]{.c7}

# [Conclusion]{.c3} {#Chapter4Reflection.xhtml_h.skncpin2wxx4 .c42}

[The reflection pattern provides a crucial mechanism for self-correction
within an agent\'s workflow, enabling iterative improvement beyond a
single-pass execution. This is achieved by creating a loop where the
system generates an output, evaluates it against specific criteria, and
then uses that evaluation to produce a refined result. This evaluation
can be performed by the agent itself (self-reflection) or, often more
effectively, by a distinct critic agent, which represents a key
architectural choice within the pattern. ]{.c7}

[While a fully autonomous, multi-step reflection process requires a
robust architecture for state management, its core principle is
effectively demonstrated in a single generate-critique-refine cycle. As
a control structure, reflection can be integrated with other
foundational patterns to construct more robust and functionally complex
agentic systems.]{.c7}

# [References]{.c3} {#Chapter4Reflection.xhtml_h.79pmfala6gox .c42}

[Here are some resources for further reading on the Reflection pattern
and related concepts:]{.c7}

1.  [Training Language Models to Self-Correct via Reinforcement
    Learning, ]{.c14} [
    [https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.12917](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.12917&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228356587250&usg=AOvVaw2vQipR0HFDHB-HkqLc_nkI){.c47}
    ]{.c46} [ ]{.c7}
2.  [LangChain Expression Language (LCEL) Documentation: ]{.c14} [
    [https://python.langchain.com/docs/introduction/](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://python.langchain.com/docs/introduction/&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228356587529&usg=AOvVaw0iu6VnG0B24WqYsoM2T_K3){.c47}
    ]{.c46} [ ]{.c7}
3.  [LangGraph Documentation:]{.c14} [
    [https://www.langchain.com/langgraph](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://www.langchain.com/langgraph&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228356587704&usg=AOvVaw3DUYjLk6A1g_-BHHcknLTP){.c47}
    ]{.c46} [ ]{.c7}
4.  [Google Agent Developer Kit (ADK) Documentation (Multi-Agent
    Systems): ]{.c14} [
    [https://google.github.io/adk-docs/agents/multi-agents/](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://google.github.io/adk-docs/agents/multi-agents/&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228356587954&usg=AOvVaw2M6UhSjMzo8_B_SI6Kqpfz){.c47}
    ]{.c46}

[]{#Chapter5ToolUse.xhtml}

[Chapter 5: Tool Use (Function Calling)]{.c2}

# [Tool Use Pattern Overview]{.c4} {#Chapter5ToolUse.xhtml_h.dkk7unq4ngk5 .c3}

[So far, we\'ve discussed agentic patterns that primarily involve
orchestrating interactions between language models and managing the flow
of information within the agent\'s internal workflow (Chaining, Routing,
