Parallelization, Reflection). However, for agents to be truly useful and
interact with the real world or external systems, they need the ability
t]{.c6} [o use ]{.c7} [Tools]{.c7} [.]{.c7}

[The Tool Use pattern, often implemented through a mechanism calle]{.c6}
[d ]{.c7} [Function Calling]{.c7} [, enables an agent to interact with
external APIs, data]{.c7} [bases, services, or even execute code. It
allows the LLM at the core of the agent to decide when and how to use a
specific external function based on the user\'s request or the current
state of the task.]{.c8}

[The process typically involves:]{.c8}

1.  [Tool Definition:]{.c12} [ External functions or capabilities are
    defined and described to the LLM. This description includes the
    function\'s purpose, its name, and the parameters it accepts, along
    with their types and descriptions.]{.c8}
2.  [LLM Decision:]{.c12} [ The LLM receives the user\'s request and the
    available tool definitions. Based on its understanding of the
    request and the tools, the LLM decides if calling one or more tools
    is necessary to fulfill the request.]{.c8}
3.  [Function Call Generation:]{.c12} [ If the LLM decides to use a
    tool, it generates a structured output (often a JSON object) that
    specifies the name of the tool to call and the arguments
    (parameters) to pass to it, extracted from the user\'s
    request.]{.c8}
4.  [Tool Execution:]{.c12} [ The agentic framework or orchestration
    layer intercepts this structured output. It identifies the requested
    tool and executes the actual external function with the provided
    arguments.]{.c8}
5.  [Observation/Result:]{.c12} [ The output or result from the tool
    execution is returned to the agent.]{.c8}
6.  [LLM Processing (Optional but common):]{.c12} [ The LLM receives the
    tool\'s output as context and uses it to formulate a final response
    to the user or decide on the next step in the workflow (which might
    involve calling another tool, reflecting, or providing a final
    answer).]{.c8}

[This pattern is fundamental because it breaks the limitations of the
LLM\'s training data and allows it to access up-to-date information,
perform calculations it can\'t do internally, interact with
user-specific data, or trigger real-world actions. Function calling is
the technical mechanism that bridges the gap between the LLM\'s
reasoning capabilities and the vast array of external functionalities
available.]{.c8}

[While \"function calling\" aptly describes invoking specific,
predefined code functions, it\'s useful to consider the more expansive
concept of \"tool calling.\" This broader term acknowledges that an
agent\'s capabilities can extend far beyond simple function execution. A
\"tool\" can be a traditional function, but it can also be a complex API
endpoint, a request to a database, or even an instruction directed at
another specialized agent. This perspective allows us to envision more
sophisticated systems where, for instance, a primary agent might
delegate a complex data analysis task to a dedicated \"analyst agent\"
or query an external knowledge base through its API. Thinking in terms
of \"tool calling\" better captures the full potential of agents to act
as orchestrators across a diverse ecosystem of digital resources and
other intelligent entities.]{.c8}

[Frameworks like LangChain, LangGraph, and Google Agent Developer Kit
(ADK) provide robust support for defining tools and integrating them
into agent workflows, often leveraging the native function calling
capabilities of modern LLMs like those in the Gemini or OpenAI series.
On the \"canvas\" of these frameworks, you define the tools and then
configure agents (typically ]{.c6} [L]{.c6} [LM ]{.c7} [Agents]{.c6} [)
to be aware of and capable of using these tools.]{.c8}

[Tool Use is a cornerstone pattern for building powerful, interactive,
and externally aware agents.]{.c8}

# [Practical Applications & Use Cases]{.c4} {#Chapter5ToolUse.xhtml_h.r4z91x18dyon .c3}

[The Tool Use pattern is applicable in virtually any scenario where an
agent needs to go beyond generating text to perform an action or
retrieve specific, dynamic information:]{.c8}

[1. Information Retrieval from External Sources:]{.c16}

[Accessing real-time data or information that is not present in the
LLM\'s training data.]{.c16}

- [Use Case:]{.c12} [ A weather agent.]{.c8}

<!-- -->

- [Tool:]{.c12} [ A weather API that takes a location and returns the
  current weather conditions.]{.c8}
- [Agent Flow:]{.c12} [ User asks, \"What\'s the weather in London?\",
  LLM identifies the need for the weather tool, calls the tool with
  \"London\", tool returns data, LLM formats the data into a
  user-friendly response.]{.c8}

[2. Interacting with Databases and APIs:]{.c16}

[Performing queries, updates, or other operations on structured
data.]{.c16}

- [Use Case:]{.c12} [ An e-commerce agent.]{.c8}

<!-- -->

- [Tools:]{.c12} [ API calls to check product inventory, get order
  status, or process payments.]{.c8}
- [Agent Flow:]{.c12} [ User asks \"Is product X in stock?\", ]{.c6}
  [LLM]{.c6} [ calls ]{.c6} [the ]{.c7} [inventory API, tool returns
  stock count, LLM tells the user the stock status.]{.c8}

[3. Performing Calculations and Data Analysis:]{.c16}

[Using external calculators, data analysis libraries, or statistical
tools.]{.c16}

- [Use Case:]{.c12} [ A financial agent.]{.c8}

<!-- -->

- [Tools:]{.c12} [ A calculator function, a stock market data API, a
  spreadsheet tool.]{.c8}
- [Agent Flow:]{.c12} [ User asks \"What\'s the current price of AAPL
  and calculate the potential profit if I bought 100 shares at \$150?\",
  LLM calls stock API, gets current price, then calls calculator tool,
  gets result, formats response.]{.c8}

[4. Sending Communications:]{.c16}

[Sending emails, messages, or making API calls to external communication
services.]{.c16}

- [Use Case:]{.c12} [ A personal assistant agent.]{.c8}

<!-- -->

- [Tool:]{.c12} [ An email sending API.]{.c8}
- [Agent Flow:]{.c12} [ User says]{.c6} [,]{.c7} [ \"Send an email to
  John about the meeting tomorrow]{.c6} [.]{.c7} [\", LLM ]{.c6} [calls
  an email]{.c7} [ tool with ]{.c6} [the ]{.c7} [recipient, subject, and
  body extracted from the request.]{.c8}

[5. Executing Code:]{.c16}

[Running code snippets in a safe environment to perform specific
tasks.]{.c16}

- [Use Case:]{.c12} [ A coding assistant agent.]{.c8}

<!-- -->

- [Tool:]{.c12} [ A code interpreter.]{.c8}
- [Agent Flow:]{.c12} [ User provides a Python snippet and asks]{.c6}
  [,]{.c7} [ \"What does this code do?\", LLM uses the interpreter tool
  to run the code and analyze its output.]{.c8}

[6. Controlling Other Systems or Devices:]{.c16}

[Interacting with smart home devices, IoT platforms, or other connected
systems.]{.c16}

- [Use Case:]{.c12} [ A smart home agent.]{.c8}

<!-- -->

- [Tool:]{.c12} [ An API to control smart lights.]{.c8}
- [Agent Flow:]{.c12} [ User says]{.c6} [,]{.c7} [ \"Turn off the living
  room lights]{.c6} [.]{.c7} [\" LLM calls the smart home tool with the
  command and target device.]{.c8}

[Tool Use is what transforms a language model from a text generator into
an agent capable of sensing, reasoning, and acting in the digital or
]{.c6} [physical world]{.c6} [ (see Fig. 1)]{.c7}

[]{.c8}

[ ![](11/GoogleDoc/images/image2.jpg){.calibre1} ]{.calibre}

[Fig.1: Some examples of an Agent using Tools]{.c8}

# [Hands-On Code Example (LangChain)]{.c4} {#Chapter5ToolUse.xhtml_h.7wgtgxpbrv84 .c3}

[The implementation of tool use within the LangChain framework is a
two-stage process. Initially, one or more tools are defined, typically
by encapsulating existing Python functions or other runnable components.
Subsequently, these tools are bound to a language model, thereby
granting the model the capability to generate a structured tool-use
request when it determines that an external function call is required to
fulfill a user\'s query.]{.c8}

[The following implementation will demonstrate this principle by first
defining a simple function to simulate an information retrieval tool.
Following this, an agent will be constructed and configured to leverage
this tool in response to user input. The execution of this example
requires the installation of the core LangChain libraries and a
model-specific provider package. Furthermore, proper authentication with
the selected language model service, typically via an API key configured
in the local environment, is a necessary prerequisite.]{.c8}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [import os, getpass]{.c28}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [import asyncio]{.c28}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [import nest_asyncio]{.c28}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [from typing import List]{.c28}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [from dotenv import load_dotenv]{.c28}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [import logging]{.c28}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [from langchain_google_genai import ChatGoogleGenerativeAI]{.c28}     |
|                                                                       |
| [from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate]{.c28}         |
|                                                                       |
| [from langchain_core.tools import tool as langchain_tool]{.c28}       |
|                                                                       |
| [from langchain.agents import create_tool_calling_agent,              |
| AgentExecutor]{.c28}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# UNCOMMENT]{.c28}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Prompt the user securely and set API keys as an environment       |
| variables]{.c28}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [os.environ\[\"GOOGLE_API_KEY\"\] = getpass.getpass(\"Enter your      |
| Google API key: \")]{.c28}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [os.environ\[\"OPENAI_API_KEY\"\] = getpass.getpass(\"Enter your      |
| OpenAI API key: \")]{.c28}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [try:]{.c28}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [  \# A model with function/tool calling capabilities is              |
| required.]{.c28}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [  llm = ChatGoogleGenerativeAI(model=\"gemini-2.0-flash\",           |
| temperature=0)]{.c28}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [  print(f\"✅ Language model initialized: {llm.model}\")]{.c28}      |
|                                                                       |
| [except Exception as e:]{.c28}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [  print(f\"🛑 Error initializing language model: {e}\")]{.c28}       |
|                                                                       |
| [  llm = None]{.c28}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- Define a Tool \-\--]{.c28}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [\@langchain_tool]{.c28}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [def search_information(query: str) -\> str:]{.c28}                   |
|                                                                       |
| [  \"\"\"]{.c28}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [  Provides factual information on a given topic. Use this tool to    |
| find answers to phrases]{.c28}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [  like \'capital of France\' or \'weather in London?\'.]{.c28}       |
|                                                                       |
| [  \"\"\"]{.c28}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [  print(f\"\\n\-\-- 🛠️ Tool Called: search_information with query:   |
| \'{query}\' \-\--\")]{.c28}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [  \# Simulate a search tool with a dictionary of predefined          |
| results.]{.c28}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [  simulated_results = {]{.c28}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [      \"weather in london\": \"The weather in London is currently    |
| cloudy with a temperature of 15°C.\",]{.c28}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [      \"capital of france\": \"The capital of France is              |
| Paris.\",]{.c28}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [      \"population of earth\": \"The estimated population of Earth   |
| is around 8 billion people.\",]{.c28}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [      \"tallest mountain\": \"Mount Everest is the tallest mountain  |
| above sea level.\",]{.c28}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [      \"default\": f\"Simulated search result for \'{query}\': No    |
| specific information found, but the topic seems interesting.\"]{.c28} |
|                                                                       |
| [  }]{.c28}                                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [  result = simulated_results.get(query.lower(),                      |
| simulated_results\[\"default\"\])]{.c28}                              |
|                                                                       |
| [  print(f\"\-\-- TOOL RESULT: {result} \-\--\")]{.c28}               |
|                                                                       |
| [  return result]{.c28}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [tools = \[search_information\]]{.c28}                                |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- Create a Tool-Calling Agent \-\--]{.c28}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [if llm:]{.c28}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [  \# This prompt template requires an \`agent_scratchpad\`           |
| placeholder for the agent\'s internal steps.]{.c28}                   |
|                                                                       |
| [  agent_prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages(\[]{.c28}          |
|                                                                       |
| [      (\"system\", \"You are a helpful assistant.\"),]{.c28}         |
|                                                                       |
| [      (\"human\", \"{input}\"),]{.c28}                               |
|                                                                       |
| [      (\"placeholder\", \"{agent_scratchpad}\"),]{.c28}              |
|                                                                       |
| [  \])]{.c28}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [  \# Create the agent, binding the LLM, tools, and prompt            |
| together.]{.c28}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [  agent = create_tool_calling_agent(llm, tools, agent_prompt)]{.c28} |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [  \# AgentExecutor is the runtime that invokes the agent and         |
| executes the chosen tools.]{.c28}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [  \# The \'tools\' argument is not needed here as they are already   |
| bound to the agent.]{.c28}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [  agent_executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, verbose=True,          |
| tools=tools)]{.c28}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [async def run_agent_with_tool(query: str):]{.c28}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [  \"\"\"Invokes the agent executor with a query and prints the final |
| response.\"\"\"]{.c28}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [  print(f\"\\n\-\-- 🏃 Running Agent with Query: \'{query}\'         |
| \-\--\")]{.c28}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [  try:]{.c28}                                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [      response = await agent_executor.ainvoke({\"input\":            |
| query})]{.c28}                                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [      print(\"\\n\-\-- ✅ Final Agent Response \-\--\")]{.c28}       |
|                                                                       |
| [      print(response\[\"output\"\])]{.c28}                           |
|                                                                       |
| [  except Exception as e:]{.c28}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [      print(f\"\\n🛑 An error occurred during agent execution:       |
| {e}\")]{.c28}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [async def main():]{.c28}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [  \"\"\"Runs all agent queries concurrently.\"\"\"]{.c28}            |
|                                                                       |
| [  tasks = \[]{.c28}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [      run_agent_with_tool(\"What is the capital of                   |
| France?\"),]{.c28}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [      run_agent_with_tool(\"What\'s the weather like in              |
| London?\"),]{.c28}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [      run_agent_with_tool(\"Tell me something about dogs.\") \#      |
| Should trigger the default tool response]{.c28}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [  \]]{.c28}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [  await asyncio.gather(\*tasks)]{.c28}                               |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [nest_asyncio.apply()]{.c28}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [asyncio.run(main())]{.c30}                                           |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[\
]{.c6} [The code sets up a tool-calling agent using the LangChain
library and the Google Gemini model. It defines a search_information
tool that simulates providing factual answers to specific queries. The
tool has predefined responses for \"weather in london,\" \"capital of
france,\" and \"population of earth,\" and a default response for other
queries. A ChatGoogleGenerativeAI model is initialized, ensuring it has
tool-calling capabilities. A ChatPromptTemplate is created to guide the
agent\'s interaction. The create_tool_calling_agent function is used to
combine the language model, tools, and prompt into an agent. An
AgentExecutor is then set up to manage the agent\'s execution and tool
invocation. The run_agent_with_tool asynchronous function is defined to
invoke the agent with a given query and print the result. The main
asynchronous function prepares multiple queries to be run concurrently.
These queries are designed to test both the specific and default
responses of the search_information tool. Finally, the
asyncio.run(main()) call executes all the agent tasks. The code includes
checks for successful LLM initialization before proceeding with agent
setup and execution.]{.c7}

# [Hands-On Code Example (CrewAI)]{.c4} {#Chapter5ToolUse.xhtml_h.b10hcyh9g36 .c3}

[This code provides a practical example of how to implement function
calling (Tools) within the CrewAI framework. It sets up a simple
scenario where an agent is equipped with a tool to look up information.
The example specifically demonstrates fetching a simulated stock price
using this agent and tool.]{.c8}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [\# pip install crewai langchain-openai]{.c28}                                          |
|                                                                                         |
| []{.c28}                                                                                |
|                                                                                         |
| [import os]{.c28}                                                                       |
|                                                                                         |
| [from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew]{.c28}                                            |
|                                                                                         |
| [from crewai.tools import tool]{.c28}                                                   |
|                                                                                         |
| [import logging]{.c28}                                                                  |
|                                                                                         |
| []{.c28}                                                                                |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# \-\-- Best Practice: Configure Logging \-\--]{.c28}                                 |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# A basic logging setup helps in debugging and tracking the crew\'s execution.]{.c28} |
|                                                                                         |
| [logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format=\'%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s -         |
| %(message)s\')]{.c28}                                                                   |
|                                                                                         |
| []{.c28}                                                                                |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# \-\-- Set up your API Key \-\--]{.c28}                                              |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# For production, it\'s recommended to use a more secure method for key               |
| management]{.c28}                                                                       |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# like environment variables loaded at runtime or a secret manager.]{.c28}            |
|                                                                                         |
| [\#]{.c28}                                                                              |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# Set the environment variable for your chosen LLM provider (e.g.,                    |
| OPENAI_API_KEY)]{.c28}                                                                  |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# os.environ\[\"OPENAI_API_KEY\"\] = \"YOUR_API_KEY\"]{.c28}                          |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# os.environ\[\"OPENAI_MODEL_NAME\"\] = \"gpt-4o\"]{.c28}                             |
|                                                                                         |
| []{.c28}                                                                                |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# \-\-- 1. Refactored Tool: Returns Clean Data \-\--]{.c28}                           |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# The tool now returns raw data (a float) or raises a standard Python error.]{.c28}   |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# This makes it more reusable and forces the agent to handle outcomes                 |
| properly.]{.c28}                                                                        |
|                                                                                         |
| [\@tool(\"Stock Price Lookup Tool\")]{.c28}                                             |
|                                                                                         |
| [def get_stock_price(ticker: str) -\> float:]{.c28}                                     |
|                                                                                         |
| [   \"\"\"]{.c28}                                                                       |
|                                                                                         |
| [   Fetches the latest simulated stock price for a given stock ticker symbol.]{.c28}    |
|                                                                                         |
| [   Returns the price as a float. Raises a ValueError if the ticker is not              |
| found.]{.c28}                                                                           |
|                                                                                         |
| [   \"\"\"]{.c28}                                                                       |
|                                                                                         |
| [   logging.info(f\"Tool Call: get_stock_price for ticker \'{ticker}\'\")]{.c28}        |
|                                                                                         |
| [   simulated_prices = {]{.c28}                                                         |
|                                                                                         |
| [       \"AAPL\": 178.15,]{.c28}                                                        |
|                                                                                         |
| [       ]{.c30} [\"GOOGL\": 1750.30,]{.c30}                                             |
|                                                                                         |
| [       \"MSFT\": 425.50,]{.c28}                                                        |
|                                                                                         |
| [   }]{.c28}                                                                            |
|                                                                                         |
| [   price = simulated_prices.get(ticker.upper())]{.c28}                                 |
|                                                                                         |
| []{.c28}                                                                                |
|                                                                                         |
| [   if price is not None:]{.c28}                                                        |
|                                                                                         |
| [       return price]{.c28}                                                             |
|                                                                                         |
| [   else:]{.c28}                                                                        |
|                                                                                         |
| [       # Raising a specific error is better than returning a string.]{.c28}            |
|                                                                                         |
| [       # The agent is equipped to handle exceptions and can decide on the next         |
| action.]{.c28}                                                                          |
|                                                                                         |
| [       raise ValueError(f\"Simulated price for ticker \'{ticker.upper()}\' not         |
| found.\")]{.c28}                                                                        |
|                                                                                         |
| []{.c28}                                                                                |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# \-\-- 2. Define the Agent \-\--]{.c28}                                              |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# The agent definition remains the same, but it will now leverage the improved        |
| tool.]{.c28}                                                                            |
|                                                                                         |
| [financial_analyst_agent = Agent(]{.c28}                                                |
|                                                                                         |
| [ role=\'Senior Financial Analyst\',]{.c28}                                             |
|                                                                                         |
| [ goal=\'Analyze stock data using provided tools and report key prices.\',]{.c28}       |
|                                                                                         |
| [ backstory=\"You are an experienced financial analyst adept at using data sources to   |
| find stock information. You provide clear, direct answers.\",]{.c28}                    |
|                                                                                         |
| [ verbose=True,]{.c28}                                                                  |
|                                                                                         |
| [ tools=\[get_stock_price\],]{.c28}                                                     |
|                                                                                         |
| [ # Allowing delegation can be useful, but is not necessary for this simple             |
| task.]{.c28}                                                                            |
|                                                                                         |
| [ allow_delegation=False,]{.c28}                                                        |
|                                                                                         |
| [)]{.c28}                                                                               |
|                                                                                         |
| []{.c28}                                                                                |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# \-\-- 3. Refined Task: Clearer Instructions and Error Handling \-\--]{.c28}         |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# The task description is more specific and guides the agent on how to react]{.c28}   |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# to both successful data retrieval and potential errors.]{.c28}                      |
|                                                                                         |
| [analyze_aapl_task = Task(]{.c28}                                                       |
|                                                                                         |
| [ description=(]{.c28}                                                                  |
|                                                                                         |
| [     \"What is the current simulated stock price for Apple (ticker: AAPL)? \"]{.c28}   |
|                                                                                         |
| [     \"Use the \'Stock Price Lookup Tool\' to find it. \"]{.c28}                       |
|                                                                                         |
| [     \"If the ticker is not found, you must report that you were unable to retrieve    |
| the price.\"]{.c28}                                                                     |
|                                                                                         |
| [ ),]{.c28}                                                                             |
|                                                                                         |
| [ expected_output=(]{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                                         |
| [     \"A single, clear sentence stating the simulated stock price for AAPL. \"]{.c28}  |
|                                                                                         |
| [     \"For example: \'The simulated stock price for AAPL is \$178.15.\' \"]{.c28}      |
|                                                                                         |
| [     \"If the price cannot be found, state that clearly.\"]{.c28}                      |
|                                                                                         |
| [ ),]{.c28}                                                                             |
|                                                                                         |
| [ agent=financial_analyst_agent,]{.c28}                                                 |
|                                                                                         |
| [)]{.c28}                                                                               |
|                                                                                         |
| []{.c28}                                                                                |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# \-\-- 4. Formulate the Crew \-\--]{.c28}                                            |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# The crew orchestrates how the agent and task work together.]{.c28}                  |
|                                                                                         |
| [financial_crew = Crew(]{.c28}                                                          |
|                                                                                         |
| [ agents=\[financial_analyst_agent\],]{.c28}                                            |
|                                                                                         |
| [ tasks=\[analyze_aapl_task\],]{.c28}                                                   |
|                                                                                         |
| [ verbose=True \# Set to False for less detailed logs in production]{.c28}              |
|                                                                                         |
| [)]{.c28}                                                                               |
|                                                                                         |
| []{.c28}                                                                                |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# \-\-- 5. Run the Crew within a Main Execution Block \-\--]{.c28}                    |
|                                                                                         |
| [\# Using a \_\_name\_\_ == \"\_\_main\_\_\": block is a standard Python best           |
| practice.]{.c28}                                                                        |
|                                                                                         |
| [def main():]{.c28}                                                                     |
|                                                                                         |
| [   \"\"\"Main function to run the crew.\"\"\"]{.c28}                                   |
|                                                                                         |
| [   # Check for API key before starting to avoid runtime errors.]{.c28}                 |
|                                                                                         |
| [   if not os.environ.get(\"OPENAI_API_KEY\"):]{.c28}                                   |
|                                                                                         |
| [       print(\"ERROR: The OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable is not set.\")]{.c28}    |
|                                                                                         |
| [       print(\"Please set it before running the script.\")]{.c28}                      |
|                                                                                         |
| [       return]{.c28}                                                                   |
|                                                                                         |
| []{.c28}                                                                                |
|                                                                                         |
| [   print(\"\\n## Starting the Financial Crew\...\")]{.c28}                             |
|                                                                                         |
| [   print(\"\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\--\")]{.c28} |
|                                                                                         |
| [  ]{.c28}                                                                              |
|                                                                                         |
| [   # The kickoff method starts the execution.]{.c28}                                   |
|                                                                                         |
| [   result = financial_crew.kickoff()]{.c28}                                            |
|                                                                                         |
| []{.c28}                                                                                |
|                                                                                         |
| [                                                                                       |
|  print(\"\\n\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\--\")]{.c28} |
|                                                                                         |
| [   print(\"## Crew execution finished.\")]{.c28}                                       |
|                                                                                         |
| [   print(\"\\nFinal Result:\\n\", result)]{.c28}                                       |
|                                                                                         |
| []{.c28}                                                                                |
|                                                                                         |
| [if \_\_name\_\_ == \"\_\_main\_\_\":]{.c28}                                            |
|                                                                                         |
| [   main()]{.c30}                                                                       |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c8}

[This code demonstrates a simple application using the Crew.ai library
to simulate a financial analysis task. It defines a custom tool,
get_stock_price, that simulates looking up stock prices for predefined
tickers. The tool is designed to return a floating-point number for
valid tickers or raise a ValueError for invalid ones. A Crew.ai Agent
named financial_analyst_agent is created with the role of a Senior
Financial Analyst. This agent is given the get_stock_price tool to
interact with. A Task is defined, analyze_aapl_task, specifically
instructing the agent to find the simulated stock price for AAPL using
the tool. The task description includes clear instructions on how to
handle both success and failure cases when using the tool. A Crew is
assembled, comprising the financial_analyst_agent and the
analyze_aapl_task. The verbose setting is enabled for both the agent and
the crew to provide detailed logging during execution. The main part of
the script runs the crew\'s task using the kickoff() method within a
standard if \_\_name\_\_ == \"\_\_main\_\_\": block. Before starting the
crew, it checks if the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable is set, which
is required for the agent to function. The result of the crew\'s
execution, which is the output of the task, is then printed to the
console. The code also includes basic logging configuration for better
tracking of the crew\'s actions and tool calls. It uses environment
variables for API key management, though it notes that more secure
methods are recommended for production environments. In short, the core
logic showcases how to define tools, agents, and tasks to create a
collaborative workflow in Crew.ai.]{.c8}

# [Hands-on code (Google ADK)]{.c4} {#Chapter5ToolUse.xhtml_h.tu940x552d8x .c3}

# [The Google Agent Developer Kit (ADK) includes a library of natively integrated tools that can be directly incorporated into an agent\'s capabilities. ]{.c33} {#Chapter5ToolUse.xhtml_h.6jfeewyc74ga .c32}

# [Google search:]{.c35} [ A primary example of such a component is the Google Search tool. This tool serves as a direct interface to the Google Search engine, equipping the agent with the functionality to perform web searches and retrieve external information.]{.c36} {#Chapter5ToolUse.xhtml_h.2p7xju3f8a0a .c34}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [from google.adk.agents import Agent]{.c37}                           |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.runners import Runner]{.c37}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.sessions import InMemorySessionService]{.c37}        |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.tools import google_search]{.c37}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.genai import types]{.c37}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [import nest_asyncio]{.c37}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [import asyncio]{.c37}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c37}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Define variables required for Session setup and Agent             |
| execution]{.c37}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [APP_NAME=\"Google Search_agent\"]{.c37}                              |
|                                                                       |
| [USER_ID=\"user1234\"]{.c37}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [SESSION_ID=\"1234\"]{.c37}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c37}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Define Agent with access to search tool]{.c37}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [root_agent = ADKAgent(]{.c37}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [  name=\"basic_search_agent\",]{.c37}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [  model=\"gemini-2.0-flash-exp\",]{.c37}                             |
|                                                                       |
| [  description=\"Agent to answer questions using Google               |
| Search.\",]{.c37}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [  instruction=\"I can answer your questions by searching the         |
| internet. Just ask me anything!\",]{.c37}                             |
|                                                                       |
| [  tools=\[google_search\] \# Google Search is a pre-built tool to    |
| perform Google searches.]{.c37}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c37}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c37}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Agent Interaction]{.c37}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [async def call_agent(query):]{.c37}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [  \"\"\"]{.c37}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [  Helper function to call the agent with a query.]{.c37}             |
|                                                                       |
| [  \"\"\"]{.c37}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c37}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [  \# Session and Runner]{.c37}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [  session_service = InMemorySessionService()]{.c37}                  |
|                                                                       |
| [  session = await session_service.create_session(app_name=APP_NAME,  |
| user_id=USER_ID, session_id=SESSION_ID)]{.c37}                        |
|                                                                       |
| [  runner = Runner(agent=root_agent, app_name=APP_NAME,               |
| session_service=session_service)]{.c37}                               |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c37}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [  content = types.Content(role=\'user\',                             |
| parts=\[types.Part(text=query)\])]{.c37}                              |
|                                                                       |
| [  events = runner.run(user_id=USER_ID, session_id=SESSION_ID,        |
| new_message=content)]{.c37}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c37}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [  for event in events:]{.c37}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [      if event.is_final_response():]{.c37}                           |
|                                                                       |
| [          final_response = event.content.parts\[0\].text]{.c37}      |
|                                                                       |
| [          print(\"Agent Response: \", final_response)]{.c37}         |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c37}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [nest_asyncio.apply()]{.c37}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c37}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [asyncio.run(call_agent(\"what\'s the latest ai news?\"))]{.c38}      |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c8}

[This code demonstrates how to create and use a basic agent powered by
the Google ADK for Python. The agent is designed to answer questions by
utilizing Google Search as a tool. First, necessary libraries from
IPython, google.adk, and google.genai are imported. Constants for the
application name, user ID, and session ID are defined. An Agent instance
named \"basic_search_agent\" is created with a description and
instructions indicating its purpose. It\'s configured to use the Google
Search tool, which is a pre-built tool provided by the ADK. An
InMemorySessionService (see Chapter 8) is initialized to manage sessions
for the agent. A new session is created for the specified application,
user, and session IDs. A Runner is instantiated, linking the created
agent with the session service. This runner is responsible for executing
the agent\'s interactions within a session. A helper function call_agent
is defined to simplify the process of sending a query to the agent and
processing the response. Inside call_agent, the user\'s query is
formatted as a types.Content object with the role \'user\'. The
runner.run method is called with the user ID, session ID, and the new
message content. The runner.run method returns a list of events
representing the agent\'s actions and responses. The code iterates
through these events to find the final response. If an event is
identified as the final response, the text content of that response is
extracted. The extracted agent response is then printed to the console.
Finally, the call_agent function is called with the query \"what\'s the
latest ai news?\" to demonstrate the agent in action.]{.c8}

[Code execution: ]{.c39} [The Google ADK features integrated components
for specialized tasks, including an environment for dynamic code
execution. The built_in_code_execution tool provides an agent with a
sandboxed Python interpreter. This allows the model to write and run
code to perform computational tasks, manipulate data structures, and
execute procedural scripts. Such functionality is critical for
addressing problems that require deterministic logic and precise ]{.c7}
[calculations]{.c7} [, which are outside the scope of probabilistic
language generation alone.]{.c8}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [import os, getpass]{.c28}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [import asyncio]{.c28}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [import nest_asyncio]{.c28}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [from typing import List]{.c28}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [from dotenv import load_dotenv]{.c28}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [import logging]{.c28}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.agents import Agent as ADKAgent, LlmAgent]{.c28}     |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.runners import Runner]{.c28}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.sessions import InMemorySessionService]{.c28}        |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.tools import google_search]{.c28}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.code_executors import BuiltInCodeExecutor]{.c28}     |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.genai import types]{.c28}                                |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Define variables required for Session setup and Agent             |
| execution]{.c28}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [APP_NAME=\"calculator\"]{.c28}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [USER_ID=\"user1234\"]{.c28}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [SESSION_ID=\"session_code_exec_async\"]{.c28}                        |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Agent Definition]{.c28}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [code_agent = LlmAgent(]{.c28}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [  name=\"calculator_agent\",]{.c28}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [  model=\"gemini-2.0-flash\",]{.c28}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [  code_executor=BuiltInCodeExecutor(),]{.c28}                        |
|                                                                       |
| [  instruction=\"\"\"You are a calculator agent.]{.c28}               |
|                                                                       |
| [  When given a mathematical expression, write and execute Python     |
| code to calculate the result.]{.c28}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [  Return only the final numerical result as plain text, without      |
| markdown or code blocks.]{.c28}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [  \"\"\",]{.c28}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [  description=\"Executes Python code to perform                      |
| calculations.\",]{.c28}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c28}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Agent Interaction (Async)]{.c28}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [async def call_agent_async(query):]{.c28}                            |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [  \# Session and Runner]{.c28}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [  session_service = InMemorySessionService()]{.c28}                  |
|                                                                       |
| [  session = await session_service.create_session(app_name=APP_NAME,  |
| user_id=USER_ID, session_id=SESSION_ID)]{.c28}                        |
|                                                                       |
| [  runner = Runner(agent=code_agent, app_name=APP_NAME,               |
| session_service=session_service)]{.c28}                               |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [  content = types.Content(role=\'user\',                             |
| parts=\[types.Part(text=query)\])]{.c28}                              |
|                                                                       |
| [  print(f\"\\n\-\-- Running Query: {query} \-\--\")]{.c28}           |
|                                                                       |
| [  final_response_text = \"No final text response captured.\"]{.c28}  |
|                                                                       |
| [  try:]{.c28}                                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [      \# Use run_async]{.c28}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [      async for event in runner.run_async(user_id=USER_ID,           |
| session_id=SESSION_ID, new_message=content):]{.c28}                   |
|                                                                       |
| [          print(f\"Event ID: {event.id}, Author:                     |
| {event.author}\")]{.c28}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [          \# \-\-- Check for specific parts FIRST \-\--]{.c28}       |
|                                                                       |
| [          \# has_specific_part = False]{.c28}                        |
|                                                                       |
| [          if event.content and event.content.parts and               |
| event.is_final_response():]{.c28}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [              for part in event.content.parts: \# Iterate through    |
| all parts]{.c28}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [                  if part.executable_code:]{.c28}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [                      \# Access the actual code string via           |
| .code]{.c28}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [                      print(f\"  Debug: Agent generated              |
| code:\\n\`\`\`python\\n{part.executable_code.code}\\n\`\`\`\")]{.c28} |
|                                                                       |
| [                      has_specific_part = True]{.c28}                |
|                                                                       |
| [                  elif part.code_execution_result:]{.c28}            |
|                                                                       |
| [                      \# Access outcome and output correctly]{.c28}  |
|                                                                       |
| [                      print(f\"  Debug: Code Execution Result:       |
| {part.code_execution_result.outcome} -                                |
| Output:\\n{part.code_execution_result.output}\")]{.c28}               |
|                                                                       |
| [                      has_specific_part = True]{.c28}                |
|                                                                       |
| [                  \# Also print any text parts found in any event    |
| for debugging]{.c28}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [                  elif part.text and not part.text.isspace():]{.c28} |
|                                                                       |
| [                      print(f\"  Text:                               |
| \'{part.text.strip()}\'\")]{.c28}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [                      \# Do not set has_specific_part=True here, as  |
| we want the final response logic below]{.c28}                         |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [              \# \-\-- Check for final response AFTER specific parts |
| \-\--]{.c28}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [              text_parts = \[part.text for part in                   |
| event.content.parts if part.text\]]{.c28}                             |
|                                                                       |
| [              final_result = \"\".join(text_parts)]{.c28}            |
|                                                                       |
| [              print(f\"==\> Final Agent Response:                    |
| {final_result}\")]{.c28}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [  except Exception as e:]{.c28}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [      print(f\"ERROR during agent run: {e}\")]{.c28}                 |
|                                                                       |
| [  print(\"-\" \* 30)]{.c28}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Main async function to run the examples]{.c28}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [async def main():]{.c28}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [  await call_agent_async(\"Calculate the value of (5 + 7) \*         |
| 3\")]{.c28}                                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [  await call_agent_async(\"What is 10 factorial?\")]{.c28}           |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c28}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Execute the main async function]{.c28}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [try:]{.c28}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [  nest_asyncio.apply()]{.c28}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [  asyncio.run(main())]{.c28}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [except RuntimeError as e:]{.c28}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [  \# Handle specific error when running asyncio.run in an already    |
| running loop (like Jupyter/Colab)]{.c28}                              |
|                                                                       |
| [  if \"cannot be called from a running event loop\" in               |
| str(e):]{.c28}                                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [      print(\"\\nRunning in an existing event loop (like             |
| Colab/Jupyter).\")]{.c28}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [      print(\"Please run \`await main()\` in a notebook cell         |
| instead.\")]{.c28}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [      \# If in an interactive environment like a notebook, you might |
| need to run:]{.c28}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [      \# await main()]{.c28}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [  else:]{.c28}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [      raise e \# Re-raise other runtime errors]{.c28}                |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c8}

[This script uses Google\'s Agent Development Kit (ADK) to create an
agent that solves mathematical problems by writing and executing Python
code. It defines an LlmAgent specifically instructed to act as a
calculator, equipping it with the built_in_code_execution tool. The
primary logic resides in the call_agent_async function, which sends a
user\'s query to the agent\'s runner and processes the resulting events.
Inside this function, an asynchronous loop iterates through events,
printing the generated Python code and its execution result for
debugging. The code carefully distinguishes between these intermediate
steps and the final event containing the numerical answer. Finally, a
main function runs the agent with two different mathematical expressions
to demonstrate its ability to perform calculations.]{.c8}

[]{.c2}

[Enterprise search: ]{.c41} [This code defines a Google ADK application
using the google.adk library in Python. It specifically uses a
VSearchAgent, which is designed to answer questions by searching a
specified Vertex AI Search datastore. The code initializes a
VSearchAgent named \"q2_strategy_vsearch_agent\", providing a
description, the model to use (\"gemini-2.0-flash-exp\"), and the ID of
the Vertex AI Search datastore. The DATASTORE_ID is expected to be set
as an environment variable. It then sets up a Runner for the agent,
using an InMemorySessionService to manage conversation history. An
asynchronous function call_vsearch_agent_async is defined to interact
with the agent. This function takes a query, constructs a message
content object, and calls the runner\'s run_async method to send the
query to the agent. The function then streams the agent\'s response back
to the console as it arrives. It also prints information about the final
response, including any source attributions from the datastore. Error
handling is included to catch exceptions during the agent\'s execution,
providing informative messages about potential issues like an incorrect
datastore ID or missing permissions. Another asynchronous function
run_vsearch_example is provided to demonstrate how to call the agent
with example queries. The main execution block checks if the
DATASTORE_ID is set and then runs the example using asyncio.run. It
includes a check to handle cases where the code is run in an environment
that already has a running event loop, like a Jupyter notebook. ]{.c16}

[]{.c16}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [import asyncio]{.c37}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.genai import types]{.c37}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk import agents]{.c37}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.runners import Runner]{.c37}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.sessions import InMemorySessionService]{.c37}        |
|                                                                       |
| [import os]{.c37}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c37}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- Configuration \-\--]{.c37}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Ensure you have set your GOOGLE_API_KEY and DATASTORE_ID          |
| environment variables]{.c37}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [\# For example:]{.c37}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [\# os.environ\[\"GOOGLE_API_KEY\"\] = \"YOUR_API_KEY\"]{.c37}        |
|                                                                       |
| [\# os.environ\[\"DATASTORE_ID\"\] = \"YOUR_DATASTORE_ID\"]{.c37}     |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c37}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [DATASTORE_ID = os.environ.get(\"DATASTORE_ID\")]{.c37}               |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c37}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- Application Constants \-\--]{.c37}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [APP_NAME = \"vsearch_app\"]{.c37}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [USER_ID = \"user_123\"  # Example User ID]{.c37}                     |
|                                                                       |
| [SESSION_ID = \"session_456\" \# Example Session ID]{.c37}            |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c37}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- Agent Definition (Updated with the newer model from the     |
| guide) \-\--]{.c37}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [vsearch_agent = agents.VSearchAgent(]{.c37}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"q2_strategy_vsearch_agent\",]{.c37}                        |
|                                                                       |
| [   description=\"Answers questions about Q2 strategy documents using |
| Vertex AI Search.\",]{.c37}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   model=\"gemini-2.0-flash-exp\", \# Updated model based on the     |
| guide\'s examples]{.c37}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   datastore_id=DATASTORE_ID,]{.c37}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [   model_parameters={\"temperature\": 0.0}]{.c37}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c37}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c37}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- Runner and Session Initialization \-\--]{.c37}              |
|                                                                       |
| [runner = Runner(]{.c37}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   agent=vsearch_agent,]{.c37}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   app_name=APP_NAME,]{.c37}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   session_service=InMemorySessionService(),]{.c37}                  |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c37}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c37}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
