| [       print(\"\\n\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\--\\n\")]{.c33}   |
|                                                                       |
| [       print(\"## Crew Final Output ##\")]{.c33}                     |
|                                                                       |
| [       print(result)]{.c33}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   except Exception as e:]{.c33}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [       print(f\"\\nAn unexpected error occurred: {e}\")]{.c33}       |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c33}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c33}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [if \_\_name\_\_ == \"\_\_main\_\_\":]{.c33}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   main()]{.c33}                                                     |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[\
]{.c5} [We will now delve into further examples within the Google ADK
framework, with particular emphasis on hierarchical, parallel, and
sequential coordination paradigms, alongside the implementation of an
agent as an operational instrument.]{.c5}

# [Hands-on Code (Google ADK)]{.c7} {#Chapter7MultiAgentCollaboration.xhtml_h.uw7xo99w3psh .c25}

[The following code example demonstrates the establishment of a
hierarchical agent structure within the Google ADK through the creation
of a parent-child relationship.]{.c5} [ The code defines two types of
agents: LlmAgent and a custom TaskExecutor agent derived from BaseAgent.
The TaskExecutor is designed for specific, non-LLM tasks and in this
example, it simply yields a \"Task finished successfully\" event. An
LlmAgent named greeter is initialized with a specified model and
instruction to act as a friendly greeter. The custom TaskExecutor is
instantiated as task_doer. A parent LlmAgent called coordinator is
created, also with a model and instructions. The coordinator\'s
instructions guide it to delegate greetings to the greeter and task
execution to the task_doer. The greeter and task_doer are added as
sub-agents to the coordinator, establishing a parent-child relationship.
The code then asserts that this relationship is correctly set up.
Finally, it prints a message indicating that the agent hierarchy has
been successfully created.]{.c4}

[]{.c4}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [from google.adk.agents import LlmAgent, BaseAgent]{.c38}             |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.agents.invocation_context import                     |
| InvocationContext]{.c38}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.events import Event]{.c38}                           |
|                                                                       |
| [from typing import AsyncGenerator]{.c38}                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Correctly implement a custom agent by extending BaseAgent]{.c38}  |
|                                                                       |
| [class TaskExecutor(BaseAgent):]{.c38}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"A specialized agent with custom, non-LLM                    |
| behavior.\"\"\"]{.c38}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [   name: str = \"TaskExecutor\"]{.c38}                               |
|                                                                       |
| [   description: str = \"Executes a predefined task.\"]{.c38}         |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   async def \_run_async_impl(self, context: InvocationContext) -\>  |
| AsyncGenerator\[Event, None\]:]{.c38}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"\"\"Custom implementation logic for the task.\"\"\"]{.c38}  |
|                                                                       |
| [       # This is where your custom logic would go.]{.c38}            |
|                                                                       |
| [       # For this example, we\'ll just yield a simple event.]{.c38}  |
|                                                                       |
| [       yield Event(author=self.name, content=\"Task finished         |
| successfully.\")]{.c38}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Define individual agents with proper initialization]{.c38}        |
|                                                                       |
| [\# LlmAgent requires a model to be specified.]{.c38}                 |
|                                                                       |
| [greeter = LlmAgent(]{.c38}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"Greeter\",]{.c38}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   model=\"gemini-2.0-flash-exp\",]{.c38}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   instruction=\"You are a friendly greeter.\"]{.c38}                |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c38}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [task_doer = TaskExecutor() \# Instantiate our concrete custom        |
| agent]{.c38}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Create a parent agent and assign its sub-agents]{.c38}            |
|                                                                       |
| [\# The parent agent\'s description and instructions should guide its |
| delegation logic.]{.c38}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [coordinator = LlmAgent(]{.c38}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"Coordinator\",]{.c38}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   model=\"gemini-2.0-flash-exp\",]{.c38}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   description=\"A coordinator that can greet users and execute      |
| tasks.\",]{.c38}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   instruction=\"When asked to greet, delegate to the Greeter. When  |
| asked to perform a task, delegate to the TaskExecutor.\",]{.c38}      |
|                                                                       |
| [   sub_agents=\[]{.c38}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       greeter,]{.c38}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [       task_doer]{.c38}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   \]]{.c38}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c38}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# The ADK framework automatically establishes the parent-child      |
| relationships.]{.c38}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [\# These assertions will pass if checked after                       |
| initialization.]{.c38}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [assert greeter.parent_agent == coordinator]{.c38}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [assert task_doer.parent_agent == coordinator]{.c38}                  |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [print(\"Agent hierarchy created successfully.\")]{.c38}              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c4}

[This code excerpt illustrates the employment of the LoopAgent within
the Google ADK framework to establish iterative workflows.]{.c5} [ The
code defines two agents: ConditionChecker and ProcessingStep.
ConditionChecker is a custom agent that checks a \"status\" value in the
session state. If the \"status\" is \"completed\", ConditionChecker
escalates an event to stop the loop. Otherwise, it yields an event to
continue the loop. ProcessingStep is an LlmAgent using the
\"gemini-2.0-flash-exp\" model. Its instruction is to perform a task and
set the session \"status\" to \"completed\" if it\'s the final step. A
LoopAgent named StatusPoller is created. StatusPoller is configured with
max_iterations=10. StatusPoller includes both ProcessingStep and an
instance of ConditionChecker as sub-agents. The LoopAgent will execute
the sub-agents sequentially for up to 10 iterations, stopping if
ConditionChecker finds the status is \"completed\".]{.c4}

[]{.c4}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [i]{.c5} [mport asyncio]{.c38}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [from typing import AsyncGenerator]{.c38}                             |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.agents import LoopAgent, LlmAgent, BaseAgent]{.c38}  |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.events import Event, EventActions]{.c38}             |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.agents.invocation_context import                     |
| InvocationContext]{.c38}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Best Practice: Define custom agents as complete, self-describing  |
| classes.]{.c38}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [class ConditionChecker(BaseAgent):]{.c38}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"A custom agent that checks for a \'completed\' status in    |
| the session state.\"\"\"]{.c38}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   name: str = \"ConditionChecker\"]{.c38}                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   description: str = \"Checks if a process is complete and signals  |
| the loop to stop.\"]{.c38}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   async def \_run_async_impl(]{.c38}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [       self, context: InvocationContext]{.c38}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   ) -\> AsyncGenerator\[Event, None\]:]{.c38}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"\"\"Checks state and yields an event to either continue or  |
| stop the loop.\"\"\"]{.c38}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [       status = context.session.state.get(\"status\",                |
| \"pending\")]{.c38}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [       is_done = (status == \"completed\")]{.c38}                    |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       if is_done:]{.c38}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [           # Escalate to terminate the loop when the condition is    |
| met.]{.c38}                                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [           yield Event(author=self.name,                             |
| actions=EventActions(escalate=True))]{.c38}                           |
|                                                                       |
| [       else:]{.c38}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [           # Yield a simple event to continue the loop.]{.c38}       |
|                                                                       |
| [           yield Event(author=self.name, content=\"Condition not     |
| met, continuing loop.\")]{.c38}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Correction: The LlmAgent must have a model and clear              |
| instructions.]{.c38}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [process_step = LlmAgent(]{.c38}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"ProcessingStep\",]{.c38}                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [   model=\"gemini-2.0-flash-exp\",]{.c38}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   instruction=\"You are a step in a longer process. Perform your    |
| task. If you are the final step, update session state by setting      |
| \'status\' to \'completed\'.\"]{.c38}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c38}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# The LoopAgent orchestrates the workflow.]{.c38}                   |
|                                                                       |
| [poller = LoopAgent(]{.c38}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"StatusPoller\",]{.c38}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   max_iterations=10,]{.c38}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   sub_agents=\[]{.c38}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       process_step,]{.c38}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [       ConditionChecker() \# Instantiating the well-defined custom   |
| agent.]{.c38}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   \]]{.c38}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c38}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# This poller will now execute \'process_step\' ]{.c38}             |
|                                                                       |
| [\# and then \'ConditionChecker\']{.c38}                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# repeatedly until the status is \'completed\' or 10 iterations     |
| ]{.c38}                                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [\# have passed.]{.c38}                                               |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c4}

[This code excerpt ]{.c5} [elucidates the SequentialAgent pattern]{.c5}
[ within the Google ADK, engineered for the construction of linear
workflows.]{.c5} [ This code defines a sequential agent pipeline using
the google.adk.agents library. The pipeline consists of two agents,
step1 and step2. step1 is named \"Step1_Fetch\" and its output will be
stored in the session state under the key \"data\". step2 is named
\"Step2_Process\" and is instructed to analyze the information stored in
session.state\[\"data\"\] and provide a summary. The SequentialAgent
named \"MyPipeline\" orchestrates the execution of these sub-agents.
When the pipeline is run with an initial input, step1 will execute
first. The response from step1 will be saved into the session state
under the key \"data\". Subsequently, step2 will execute, utilizing the
information that step1 placed into the state as per its instruction.
This structure allows for building workflows where the output of one
agent becomes the input for the next. This is a common pattern in
creating multi-step AI or data processing pipelines. ]{.c4}

[]{.c4}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [from google.adk.agents import SequentialAgent, Agent]{.c38}          |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# This agent\'s output will be saved to                             |
| session.state\[\"data\"\]]{.c38}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [step1 = Agent(name=\"Step1_Fetch\", output_key=\"data\")]{.c38}      |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# This agent will use the data from the previous step.]{.c38}       |
|                                                                       |
| [\# We instruct it on how to find and use this data.]{.c38}           |
|                                                                       |
| [step2 = Agent(]{.c38}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"Step2_Process\",]{.c38}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [   instruction=\"Analyze the information found in state\[\'data\'\]  |
| and provide a summary.\"]{.c38}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c38}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [pipeline = SequentialAgent(]{.c38}                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"MyPipeline\",]{.c38}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   sub_agents=\[step1, step2\]]{.c38}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c38}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# When the pipeline is run with an initial input, Step1 will        |
| execute,]{.c38}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [\# its response will be stored in session.state\[\"data\"\], and     |
| then]{.c38}                                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Step2 will execute, using the information from the state as       |
| instructed.]{.c38}                                                    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c4}

[The following code example illustrates the ParallelAgent pattern within
the Google ADK, which facilitates the concurrent execution of multiple
agent tasks.]{.c5} [ The data_gatherer is designed to run two sub-agents
concurrently: weather_fetcher and news_fetcher. The weather_fetcher
agent is instructed to get the weather for a given location and store
the result in session.state\[\"weather_data\"\]. Similarly, the
news_fetcher agent is instructed to retrieve the top news story for a
given topic and store it in session.state\[\"news_data\"\]. Each
sub-agent is configured to use the \"gemini-2.0-flash-exp\" model. The
ParallelAgent orchestrates the execution of these sub-agents, allowing
them to work in parallel. The results from both weather_fetcher and
news_fetcher would be gathered and stored in the session state. Finally,
the example shows how to access the collected weather and news data from
the final_state after the agent\'s execution is complete.]{.c4}

[]{.c4}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [from google.adk.agents import Agent, ParallelAgent]{.c38}            |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# It\'s better to define the fetching logic as tools for the        |
| agents]{.c38}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [\# For simplicity in this example, we\'ll embed the logic in the     |
| agent\'s instruction.]{.c38}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [\# In a real-world scenario, you would use tools.]{.c38}             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Define the individual agents that will run in parallel]{.c38}     |
|                                                                       |
| [weather_fetcher = Agent(]{.c38}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"weather_fetcher\",]{.c38}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [   model=\"gemini-2.0-flash-exp\",]{.c38}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   instruction=\"Fetch the weather for the given location and return |
| only the weather report.\",]{.c38}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [   output_key=\"weather_data\"  # The result will be stored in       |
| session.state\[\"weather_data\"\]]{.c38}                              |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c38}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [news_fetcher = Agent(]{.c38}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"news_fetcher\",]{.c38}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   model=\"gemini-2.0-flash-exp\",]{.c38}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   instruction=\"Fetch the top news story for the given topic and    |
| return only that story.\",]{.c38}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   output_key=\"news_data\"      # The result will be stored in      |
| session.state\[\"news_data\"\]]{.c38}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c38}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Create the ParallelAgent to orchestrate the sub-agents]{.c38}     |
|                                                                       |
| [data_gatherer = ParallelAgent(]{.c38}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"data_gatherer\",]{.c38}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [   sub_agents=\[]{.c38}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       weather_fetcher,]{.c38}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [       news_fetcher]{.c38}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   \]]{.c38}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c38}                                                             |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c4}

[The provided code segment exemplifies the \"Agent as a Tool\" paradigm
within the Google ADK, enabling an agent to utilize the capabilities of
another agent in a manner analogous to function invocation.]{.c5}
[ ]{.c5} [Specifically, ]{.c5} [t]{.c5} [he code defines an image
generation system using Google\'s LlmAgent and AgentTool classes. It
consists of two agents: a parent artist_agent and a sub-agent
image_generator_agent. The generate_image function is a simple tool that
simulates image creation, returning mock image data. The
image_generator_agent is responsible for using this tool based on a text
prompt it receives. The artist_agent\'s role is to first invent a
creative image prompt. It then calls the image_generator_agent through
an AgentTool wrapper. The AgentTool acts as a bridge, allowing one agent
to use another agent as a tool. When the artist_agent calls the
image_tool, the AgentTool invokes the image_generator_agent with the
artist\'s invented prompt. The image_generator_agent then uses the
generate_image function with that prompt. Finally, the generated image
(or mock data) is returned back up through the agents. This architecture
demonstrates a layered agent system where a higher-level agent
orchestrates a lower-level, specialized agent to perform a task.]{.c4}

[]{.c4}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [from google.adk.agents import LlmAgent]{.c38}                        |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.tools import agent_tool]{.c38}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.genai import types]{.c38}                                |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# 1. A simple function tool for the core capability.]{.c38}         |
|                                                                       |
| [\# This follows the best practice of separating actions from         |
| reasoning.]{.c38}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [def generate_image(prompt: str) -\> dict:]{.c38}                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"]{.c38}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   Generates an image based on a textual prompt.]{.c38}              |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   Args:]{.c38}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       prompt: A detailed description of the image to                |
| generate.]{.c38}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   Returns:]{.c38}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [       A dictionary with the status and the generated image          |
| bytes.]{.c38}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"]{.c38}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   print(f\"TOOL: Generating image for prompt:                       |
| \'{prompt}\'\")]{.c38}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [   # In a real implementation, this would call an image generation   |
| API.]{.c38}                                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   # For this example, we return mock image data.]{.c38}             |
|                                                                       |
| [   mock_image_bytes =                                                |
| b\"mock_image_data_for_a_cat_wearing_a_hat\"]{.c38}                   |
|                                                                       |
| [   return {]{.c38}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"status\": \"success\",]{.c38}                               |
|                                                                       |
| [       # The tool returns the raw bytes, the agent will handle the   |
| Part creation.]{.c38}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"image_bytes\": mock_image_bytes,]{.c38}                     |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"mime_type\": \"image/png\"]{.c38}                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   }]{.c38}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# 2. Refactor the ImageGeneratorAgent into an LlmAgent.]{.c38}      |
|                                                                       |
| [\# It now correctly uses the input passed to it.]{.c38}              |
|                                                                       |
| [image_generator_agent = LlmAgent(]{.c38}                             |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"ImageGen\",]{.c38}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   model=\"gemini-2.0-flash\",]{.c38}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [   description=\"Generates an image based on a detailed text         |
| prompt.\",]{.c38}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   instruction=(]{.c38}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"You are an image generation specialist. Your task is to     |
| take the user\'s request \"]{.c38}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"and use the \`generate_image\` tool to create the image.    |
| \"]{.c38}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"The user\'s entire request should be used as the \'prompt\' |
| argument for the tool. \"]{.c38}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"After the tool returns the image bytes, you MUST output the |
| image.\"]{.c38}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   ),]{.c38}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   tools=\[generate_image\]]{.c38}                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c38}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# 3. Wrap the corrected agent in an AgentTool.]{.c38}               |
|                                                                       |
| [\# The description here is what the parent agent sees.]{.c38}        |
|                                                                       |
| [image_tool = agent_tool.AgentTool(]{.c38}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   agent=image_generator_agent,]{.c38}                               |
|                                                                       |
| [   description=\"Use this tool to generate an image. The input       |
| should be a descriptive prompt of the desired image.\"]{.c38}         |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c38}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c38}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# 4. The parent agent remains unchanged. Its logic was              |
| correct.]{.c38}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [artist_agent = LlmAgent(]{.c38}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"Artist\",]{.c38}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   model=\"gemini-2.0-flash\",]{.c38}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [   instruction=(]{.c38}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"You are a creative artist. First, invent a creative and     |
| descriptive prompt for an image. \"]{.c38}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"Then, use the \`ImageGen\` tool to generate the image using |
| your prompt.\"]{.c38}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [   ),]{.c38}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   tools=\[image_tool\]]{.c38}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c38}                                                             |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c39}

# [At a Glance]{.c7} {#Chapter7MultiAgentCollaboration.xhtml_h.6x9sek3jvzvp .c40}

[What:]{.c42} [ Complex problems often exceed the capabilities of a
single, monolithic LLM-based agent. A solitary agent may lack the
diverse, specialized skills or access to the specific tools needed to
address all parts of a multifaceted task. This limitation creates a
bottleneck, reducing the system\'s overall effectiveness and
scalability. As a result, tackling sophisticated, multi-domain
objectives becomes inefficient and can lead to incomplete or suboptimal
outcomes.]{.c23}

[Why:]{.c42} [ The Multi-Agent Collaboration pattern offers a
standardized solution by creating a system of multiple, cooperating
agents. A complex problem is broken down into smaller, more manageable
sub-problems. Each sub-problem is then assigned to a specialized agent
with the precise tools and capabilities required to solve it. These
agents work together through defined communication protocols and
interaction models like sequential handoffs, parallel workstreams, or
hierarchical delegation. This agentic, distributed approach creates a
synergistic effect, allowing the group to achieve outcomes that would be
impossible for any single agent.]{.c23}

[Rule of thumb:]{.c42} [ Use this pattern when a task is too complex for
a single agent and can be decomposed into distinct sub-tasks requiring
specialized skills or tools. It is ideal for problems that benefit from
diverse expertise, parallel processing, or a structured workflow with
multiple stages, such as complex research and analysis, software
development, or creative content generation.]{.c23}

[Visual summary]{.c22}

[ ![](13/GoogleDoc/images/image3.jpg){.calibre4} ]{.calibre3}

[Fig.3: Multi-Agent design pattern]{.c23}

[]{.c2}

# [Key Takeaways]{.c7} {#Chapter7MultiAgentCollaboration.xhtml_h.r9hw4l47ilaz .c6}

- [Multi-agent collaboration involves multiple agents working together
  to achieve a common goal.]{.c4}
- [This pattern leverages specialized roles, distributed tasks, and
  ]{.c5} [inter-agent communication.]{.c5}
- [Collaboration can take forms like sequential handoffs, parallel
  processing, debate, or hierarchical structures.]{.c4}
- [This pattern is ideal for complex problems requiring diverse
  expertise or multiple distinct stages.]{.c5}

# [Conclusion ]{.c7} {#Chapter7MultiAgentCollaboration.xhtml_h.4moxv1bcsrol .c25}

[This chapter explored the Multi-Agent Collaboration pattern,
demonstrating the benefits of orchestrating multiple specialized agents
within systems.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [We examined various collaboration
models, emphasizing the pattern\'s essential role in addressing complex,
multifaceted problems across diverse domains.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4}
[Understanding agent collaboration naturally leads to an inquiry into
their interactions with the external environment.]{.c4}

# [References]{.c7} {#Chapter7MultiAgentCollaboration.xhtml_h.tv4ixjhmqf96 .c25}

1.  [Multi-Agent Collaboration Mechanisms: A Survey of LLMs,
    ]{.calibre6} [
    [https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.06322](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.06322&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228394958984&usg=AOvVaw25OZDXHB_w8RvlfTnHdHeL){.c47}
    ]{.c46} [ ]{.c2}
2.  [Multi-Agent System --- The Power of Collaboration, ]{.calibre6} [
    [https://aravindakumar.medium.com/introducing-multi-agent-frameworks-the-power-of-collaboration-e9db31bba1b6](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://aravindakumar.medium.com/introducing-multi-agent-frameworks-the-power-of-collaboration-e9db31bba1b6&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228394959611&usg=AOvVaw3_X1CAS_Y4yVBkv_5mOBTA){.c47}
    ]{.c46} [ ]{.calibre6}

[]{#Chapter8MemoryManagement.xhtml}

[Chapter]{.calibre} [ 8: Memory Management ]{.c2}

[Effective memory management is crucial for intelligent agents to retain
information.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [Agents require different types of memory,
much like humans, to operate efficiently.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [This chapter
delves into memory management, specifically addressing the immediate
(short-term) and persistent (long-term) memory requirements of
agents.]{.c4}

[In agent systems, memory refers to an agent\'s ability to retain and
utilize information from past interactions, observations, and learning
experiences.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [This capability allows agents to make
informed decisions, maintain conversational context, and improve over
time.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [Agent memory is generally categorized into two
main types:]{.c4}

- [Short-Term Memory (Contextual Memory):]{.c7} [ ]{.c8} [Similar to
  working memory, this holds information currently being processed or
  recently accessed. For agents using large language models (LLMs),
  short-term memory primarily exists within the context window. This
  window contains recent messages, agent replies, tool usage results,
  and agent reflections from the current interaction, all of which
  inform the LLM\'s subsequent responses and actions. The context window
  has a limited capacity, restricting the amount of recent information
  an agent can directly access. Efficient short-term memory management
  involves keeping the most relevant information within this limited
  space, possibly through techniques like summarizing older conversation
  segments or emphasizing key details. The advent of models with \'long
  context\' windows simply expands the size of this short-term memory,
  allowing more information to be held within a single interaction.
  However, this context is still ephemeral and is lost once the session
  concludes, and it can be costly and inefficient to process every time.
  Consequently, agents require separate memory types to achieve true
  persistence, recall information from past interactions, and build a
  lasting knowledge base.]{.c8}
- [Long-Term Memory (Persistent Memory):]{.c7} [ This acts as a
  repository for information agents need to retain across various ]{.c8}
  [interactions]{.c8} [, tasks, or extended periods, akin to long-term
  knowledge bases.]{.c8} [ ]{.c8} [Data is typically stored outside the
  agent\'s immediate processing environment, often in databases,
  knowledge graphs, or vector databases.]{.c8} [ ]{.c8} [In vector
  databases, information is converted into numerical vectors and stored,
  enabling agents to retrieve data based on semantic similarity rather
  than exact keyword matches, a process known as semantic search.]{.c8}
  [ ]{.c8} [When an agent needs information from long-term memory, it
  queries the external storage, retrieves relevant data, and integrates
  it into the short-term context for immediate use, thus combining prior
  knowledge with the current interaction.]{.c8}

# [Practical Applications & Use Cases]{.c10} {#Chapter8MemoryManagement.xhtml_h.72vrsxlg472o .c9}

[Memory management is vital for agents to track information and perform
intelligently over time.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [This is essential for agents to
surpass basic question-answering capabilities.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4}
[Applications include:]{.c4}

- [Chatbots and Conversational AI:]{.c11} [ Maintaining conversation
  flow relies on short-term memory.]{.c12} [ ]{.c12} [Chatbots require
  remembering prior user inputs to provide coherent responses.]{.c12}
  [ ]{.c12} [Long-term memory enables chatbots to recall user
  preferences, past issues, or prior discussions, offering personalized
  and continuous interactions.]{.c12}
- [Task-Oriented Agents:]{.c11} [ Agents managing multi-step tasks need
  short-term memory to track previous steps, current progress, and
  overall goals.]{.c12} [ ]{.c12} [This information might reside in the
  task\'s context or temporary storage.]{.c12} [ ]{.c12} [Long-term
  memory is crucial for accessing specific user-related data not in the
  immediate context.]{.c12}
- [Personalized Experiences:]{.c11} [ Agents offering tailored
  interactions utilize long-term memory to store and retrieve user
  preferences, past behaviors, and personal information.]{.c12}
  [ ]{.c12} [This allows agents to adapt their responses and
  suggestions.]{.c12}
- [Learning and Improvement:]{.c11} [ Agents can refine their
  performance by learning from past interactions.]{.c12} [ ]{.c12}
  [Successful strategies, mistakes, and new information are stored in
  long-term memory, facilitating future adaptations.]{.c12} [ ]{.c12}
  [Reinforcement learning agents store learned strategies or
  knowledge]{.c12} [ in]{.c12} [ this way.]{.c12}
- [Information Retrieval (RAG):]{.c11} [ Agents designed for answering
  questions access a knowledge base, their long-term memory, often
  implemented ]{.c12} [with]{.c12} [in Retrieval Augmented Generation
  (RAG). The agent retrieves relevant documents or data to inform its
  responses.]{.c12}
- [Autonomous Systems:]{.c11} [ Robots or self-driving cars require
  memory for maps, routes, object locations, and learned
  behaviors.]{.c12} [ ]{.c12} [This involves short-term memory for
  immediate surroundings and long-term memory for general environmental
  knowledge.]{.c12}

[Memory enables agents to maintain history, learn, personalize
interactions, and manage complex, time-dependent problems.]{.c12}

[]{.c15}

# [Hands-On Code]{.calibre} [: ]{.calibre} [Memory ]{.calibre} [Management]{.calibre} [ in Google Agent Developer Kit (ADK)]{.c10} {#Chapter8MemoryManagement.xhtml_h.762pnixlg17e .c16}

[The Google Agent Developer Kit (ADK) offers a structured method for
managing context and memory, including components for practical
application.]{.c12} [ ]{.c12} [A solid grasp of ADK\'s Session, State,
and Memory is vital for building agents that need to retain
information.]{.c12}

[Just as in human interactions, agents require the ability to recall
previous exchanges to conduct coherent and natural conversations.]{.c12}
[ ]{.c12} [ADK simplifies context management through three core concepts
and their associated services.]{.c12}

[Every interaction with an agent can be considered a unique conversation
thread.]{.c12} [ ]{.c12} [Agents might need to access data from earlier
interactions.]{.c12} [ ]{.c12} [ADK structures this as follows:]{.c12}

- [Session:]{.c7} [ An individual chat thread that logs messages and
  actions (Events) for that specific interaction, also storing temporary
  data (State) relevant to that conversation.]{.c8}
- [State (session.state):]{.c7} [ Data stored within a Session,
  containing information relevant only to the current, active chat
  thread.]{.c8}
- [Memory:]{.c7} [ A searchable repository of information sourced from
  various past chats or external sources, serving as a resource for data
  retrieval beyond the immediate conversation.]{.c8}

[ADK provides dedicated services for managing critical components
essential for building complex, stateful, and context-aware
agents.]{.c8} [ ]{.c8} [The SessionService manages chat threads (Session
objects) by handling their initiation, recording, and termination, while
the MemoryService oversees the storage and retrieval of long-term
knowledge (Memory).]{.c8}

[Both the SessionService and MemoryService offer various configuration
options, allowing users to choose storage methods based on application
needs.]{.c8} [ ]{.c8} [In-memory options are available for testing
purposes, though data will not persist across restarts.]{.c8} [ ]{.c8}
[For persistent storage and scalability, ADK also supports database and
cloud-based services.]{.c8}

[]{.c8}

## [Session: Keeping Track of Each Chat]{.c10} {#Chapter8MemoryManagement.xhtml_h.pgdps7e7qlw9 .c19}

[A Session object in ADK is designed to track and manage individual chat
threads.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [Upon initiation of a conversation with an
agent, the SessionService generates a Session object, represented as
\`google.adk.sessions.Session\`.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [This object
encapsulates all data relevant to a specific conversation thread,
including unique identifiers (id, app_name, user_id), a chronological
record of events as Event objects, a storage area for session-specific
temporary data known as state, and a timestamp indicating the last
update (last_update_time).]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [Developers typically interact
with Session objects indirectly through the SessionService.]{.c4}
[ ]{.c4} [The SessionService is responsible for managing the lifecycle
of conversation sessions, which includes initiating new sessions,
resuming previous sessions, recording session activity (including state
updates), identifying active sessions, and managing the removal of
session data.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [The ADK provides several SessionService
implementations with varying storage mechanisms for session history and
temporary data, such as the InMemorySessionService, which is suitable
for testing but does not provide data persistence across application
restarts.]{.c4}

[]{.c15}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [\# Example: Using InMemorySessionService]{.c25}                      |
|                                                                       |
| [\# This is suitable for local development and testing where data     |
| ]{.c25}                                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [\# persistence across application restarts ]{.c26} [is]{.c26} [ not  |
| required.]{.c25}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.sessions import InMemorySessionService]{.c25}        |
|                                                                       |
| [session_service = InMemorySessionService()]{.c25}                    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c15}

[Then there\'s DatabaseSessionService if you want reliable saving to a
database you manage. ]{.c15}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [\# Example: Using DatabaseSessionService]{.c25}                      |
|                                                                       |
| [\# This is suitable for production or development requiring          |
| persistent storage.]{.c25}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [\# You need to configure a database URL (e.g., for SQLite,           |
| PostgreSQL, etc.).]{.c25}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Requires: pip install google-adk\[sqlalchemy\] and a database     |
| driver (e.g., psycopg2 for PostgreSQL)]{.c25}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.sessions import DatabaseSessionService]{.c25}        |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Example using a local SQLite file:]{.c25}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [db_url = \"sqlite:///./my_agent_data.db\"]{.c25}                     |
|                                                                       |
| [session_service = DatabaseSessionService(db_url=db_url)]{.c25}       |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c15}

[]{.c15}

[]{.c15}

[Besides, there\'s VertexAiSessionService which uses Vertex AI
infrastructure for scalable production on Google Cloud.]{.c15}

+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [\# Example: Using VertexAiSessionService]{.c25}                                       |
|                                                                                        |
| [\# This is suitable for scalable production on Google Cloud Platform,                 |
| leveraging]{.c25}                                                                      |
|                                                                                        |
| [\# Vertex AI infrastructure for session management.]{.c25}                            |
|                                                                                        |
| [\# Requires: pip install google-adk\[vertexai\] and GCP setup/authentication]{.c25}   |
|                                                                                        |
| [from google.adk.sessions import VertexAiSessionService]{.c25}                         |
|                                                                                        |
| []{.c25}                                                                               |
|                                                                                        |
| [PROJECT_ID = \"your-gcp-project-id\" \# Replace with your GCP project ID]{.c25}       |
|                                                                                        |
| [LOCATION = \"us-central1\" \# Replace with your desired GCP location]{.c25}           |
|                                                                                        |
| [\# The app_name used with this service should correspond to the Reasoning Engine ID   |
| or name]{.c25}                                                                         |
|                                                                                        |
| [REASONING_ENGINE_APP_NAME =                                                           |
| \"projects/your-gcp-project-id/locations/us-central1/reasoningEngines/your-engine-id\" |
| \# Replace with your Reasoning Engine resource name]{.c25}                             |
|                                                                                        |
| []{.c25}                                                                               |
|                                                                                        |
| [session_service = VertexAiSessionService(project=PROJECT_ID,                          |
| location=LOCATION)]{.c25}                                                              |
|                                                                                        |
| [\# When using this service, pass REASONING_ENGINE_APP_NAME to service methods:]{.c25} |
|                                                                                        |
| [\# session_service.create_session(app_name=REASONING_ENGINE_APP_NAME, \...)]{.c25}    |
|                                                                                        |
| [\# session_service.get_session(app_name=REASONING_ENGINE_APP_NAME, \...)]{.c25}       |
|                                                                                        |
| [\# session_service.append_event(session, event,                                       |
| app_name=REASONING_ENGINE_APP_NAME)]{.c25}                                             |
|                                                                                        |
| [\# session_service.delete_session(app_name=REASONING_ENGINE_APP_NAME, \...)]{.c25}    |
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c15}

[Choosing an appropriate SessionService is crucial as it determines how
the agent\'s interaction history and temporary data are stored and their
persistence.]{.c4}

[Each message exchange involves a cyclical process: A message is
received, the Runner retrieves or establishes a Session using the
SessionService, the agent processes the message using the Session\'s
context (state and historical interactions), the agent generates a
response and may update the state, the Runner encapsulates this as an
Event, and the session_service.append_event method records the new event
and updates the state in storage.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [The Session then
awaits the next message.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [Ideally, the delete_session
method is employed to terminate the session when the interaction
concludes.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [This process illustrates how the
SessionService maintains continuity by managing the Session-specific
history and temporary data.]{.c4}

## [State: The Session\'s Scratchpad]{.c10} {#Chapter8MemoryManagement.xhtml_h.k6kz4ifkb842 .c30}

[In the ADK, each Session, representing a chat thread, includes a state
component akin to an agent\'s temporary working memory for the duration
of that specific conversation.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [While session.events logs
the entire chat history, session.state stores and updates dynamic data
points relevant to the active chat.]{.c4}

[Fundamentally, session.state operates as a dictionary, storing data as
key-value pairs.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [Its core function is to enable the
agent to retain and manage details essential for coherent dialogue, such
as user preferences, task progress, incremental data collection, or
conditional flags influencing subsequent agent actions.]{.c4}

[The state's structure comprises string keys paired with values of
serializable Python types, including strings, numbers, booleans, lists,
and dictionaries containing these basic types.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [State is
dynamic, evolving throughout the conversation.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [The
permanence of these changes depends on the configured
SessionService.]{.c4}

[State organization can be achieved using key prefixes to define data
scope and persistence.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [Keys without prefixes are
session-specific.]{.c4} [ ]{.c15}

- [The user: prefix associates data with a user ID across all
  sessions.]{.c4} [ ]{.c15}
- [The app: prefix designates data shared among all users of the
  application.]{.c4} [ ]{.c15}
- [The temp: prefix indicates data valid only for the current processing
  turn and is not persistently stored.]{.c4} [ ]{.c15}

[The agent accesses all state data through a single session.state
dictionary.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [The SessionService handles data retrieval,
merging, and persistence.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [State should be updated upon
adding an Event to the session history via
session_service.append_event().]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [This ensures accurate
tracking, proper saving in persistent services, and safe handling of
state changes.]{.c4}

[]{.c15}

1.  [The Simple Way: Using output_key (for Agent Text Replies):]{.c36}
    [ This is the easiest method if you just want to save your agent\'s
    final text response directly into the state. When you set up your
    LlmAgent, just tell it the output_key you want to use. The Runner
    sees this and automatically creates the necessary actions to save
    the response to the state when it appends the event.]{.c37}
    [ ]{.calibre} [Let\'s look at a code example demonstrating state
    update via output_key.]{.c37}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [\# Import necessary classes from the Google Agent Developer Kit      |
| (ADK)]{.c39}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.agents import LlmAgent]{.c39}                        |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.sessions import InMemorySessionService,              |
| Session]{.c39}                                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.runners import Runner]{.c39}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.genai.types import Content, Part]{.c39}                  |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c39}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Define an LlmAgent with an output_key.]{.c39}                     |
|                                                                       |
| [greeting_agent = LlmAgent(]{.c39}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"Greeter\",]{.c39}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   model=\"gemini-2.0-flash\",]{.c39}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [   instruction=\"Generate a short, friendly greeting.\",]{.c39}      |
|                                                                       |
| [   output_key=\"last_greeting\"]{.c39}                               |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c39}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c39}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- Setup Runner and Session \-\--]{.c39}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [app_name, user_id, session_id = \"state_app\", \"user1\",            |
| \"session1\"]{.c39}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [session_service = InMemorySessionService()]{.c39}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [runner = Runner(]{.c39}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   agent=greeting_agent,]{.c39}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   app_name=app_name,]{.c39}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   session_service=session_service]{.c39}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c39}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [session = session_service.create_session(]{.c39}                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   app_name=app_name,]{.c39}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   user_id=user_id,]{.c39}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   session_id=session_id]{.c39}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c39}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c39}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [print(f\"Initial state: {session.state}\")]{.c39}                    |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c39}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- Run the Agent \-\--]{.c39}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [user_message = Content(parts=\[Part(text=\"Hello\")\])]{.c39}        |
|                                                                       |
| [print(\"\\n\-\-- Running the agent \-\--\")]{.c39}                   |
|                                                                       |
| [for event in runner.run(]{.c39}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   user_id=user_id,]{.c39}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   session_id=session_id,]{.c39}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   new_message=user_message]{.c39}                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [):]{.c39}                                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   if event.is_final_response():]{.c39}                              |
|                                                                       |
| [     print(\"Agent responded.\")]{.c39}                              |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c39}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- Check Updated State \-\--]{.c39}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Correctly check the state \*after\* the runner has finished       |
| processing all events.]{.c39}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [updated_session = session_service.get_session(app_name, user_id,     |
| session_id)]{.c39}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [print(f\"\\nState after agent run: {updated_session.state}\")]{.c39} |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c15}

[Behind the scenes, the Runner sees your output_key and automatically
creates the necessary actions with a state_delta when it calls
append_event.]{.c37}

2.  [The Standard Way: Using EventActions.state_delta (for More
    Complicated Updates):]{.c36} [ For times when you need to do more
    complex things -- like updating several keys at once, saving things
    that aren\'t just text, targeting specific scopes like user: or
    app:, or making updates that aren\'t tied to the agent\'s final text
    reply -- you\'ll manually build a dictionary of your state changes
    (the state_delta) and include it within the EventActions of the
    Event you\'re appending. Let]{.c37} [\'s look at one example:]{.c15}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [import time]{.c25}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.tools.tool_context import ToolContext]{.c25}         |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.sessions import InMemorySessionService]{.c25}        |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c25}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- Define the Recommended Tool-Based Approach \-\--]{.c25}     |
|                                                                       |
| [def log_user_login(tool_context: ToolContext) -\> dict:]{.c25}       |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"]{.c25}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   Updates the session state upon a user login event.]{.c25}         |
|                                                                       |
| [   This tool encapsulates all state changes related to a user        |
| login.]{.c25}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   Args:]{.c25}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       tool_context: Automatically provided by ADK, gives access to  |
| session state.]{.c25}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [   Returns:]{.c25}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [       A dictionary confirming the action was successful.]{.c25}     |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"]{.c25}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   # Access the state directly through the provided context.]{.c25}  |
|                                                                       |
| [   state = tool_context.state]{.c25}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [  ]{.c25}                                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   # Get current values or defaults, then update the state.]{.c25}   |
|                                                                       |
| [   # This is much cleaner and co-locates the logic.]{.c25}           |
|                                                                       |
| [   login_count = state.get(\"user:login_count\", 0) + 1]{.c25}       |
|                                                                       |
| [   state\[\"user:login_count\"\] = login_count]{.c25}                |
|                                                                       |
| [   state\[\"task_status\"\] = \"active\"]{.c25}                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   state\[\"user:last_login_ts\"\] = time.time()]{.c25}              |
|                                                                       |
| [   state\[\"temp:validation_needed\"\] = True]{.c25}                 |
|                                                                       |
| [  ]{.c25}                                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   print(\"State updated from within the \`log_user_login\`          |
| tool.\")]{.c25}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [  ]{.c25}                                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   return {]{.c25}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"status\": \"success\",]{.c25}                               |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"message\": f\"User login tracked. Total logins:             |
| {login_count}.\"]{.c25}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [   }]{.c25}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c25}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- Demonstration of Usage \-\--]{.c25}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [\# In a real application, an LLM Agent would decide to call this     |
| tool.]{.c25}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Here, we simulate a direct call for demonstration                 |
| purposes.]{.c25}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c25}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# 1. Setup]{.c25}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [session_service = InMemorySessionService()]{.c25}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [app_name, user_id, session_id = \"state_app_tool\", \"user3\",       |
| \"session3\"]{.c25}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [session = session_service.create_session(]{.c25}                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   app_name=app_name,]{.c25}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   user_id=user_id,]{.c25}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   session_id=session_id,]{.c25}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   state={\"user:login_count\": 0, \"task_status\": \"idle\"}]{.c25} |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c25}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [print(f\"Initial state: {session.state}\")]{.c25}                    |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c25}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# 2. Simulate a tool call (in a real app, the ADK Runner does       |
| this)]{.c25}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [\# We create a ToolContext manually just for this standalone         |
| example.]{.c25}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.tools.tool_context import InvocationContext]{.c25}   |
|                                                                       |
| [mock_context = ToolContext(]{.c25}                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [   invocation_context=InvocationContext(]{.c25}                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       app_name=app_name, user_id=user_id,                           |
