[]{.c4}

[]{#Chapter15InterAgentCommunication%28A2A%29.xhtml}

[Chapter 15: Inter-Agent Communication (A2A)]{.calibre}

[Individual AI agents often face limitations when tackling complex,
multifaceted problems, even with advanced capabilities.]{.c3} [ ]{.c3}
[To overcome this, Inter-Agent Communication (A2A) enables diverse AI
agents, potentially built with different frameworks, to collaborate
effectively.]{.c3} [ ]{.c3} [This collaboration involves seamless
coordination, task delegation, and information exchange.]{.c3}

[Google\'s A2A protocol is an open  standard designed to facilitate this
universal communication.]{.c3} [ ]{.c3} [This chapter will explore A2A,
its practical applications, and its implementation within the Google
ADK.]{.c3}

# [Inter-Agent Communication Pattern Overview]{.c5} {#Chapter15InterAgentCommunication(A2A).xhtml_h.4an9dtk4pxg2 .c4}

[The Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol is an open standard designed to enable
communication and collaboration between different AI agent
frameworks.]{.c7} [ ]{.c7} [It ensures interoperability, allowing AI
agents developed with technologies like LangGraph, CrewAI, or Google ADK
to work together regardless of their origin or framework
differences.]{.c7}

[]{.c7}

[A2A is supported by a range of technology companies and service
providers, including Atlassian, Box, LangChain, MongoDB, Salesforce,
SAP, and ServiceNow.]{.c7} [ ]{.c7} [Microsoft plans to integrate A2A
into Azure AI Foundry and Copilot Studio, demonstrating its commitment
to open protocols.]{.c7} [ ]{.c7} [Additionally, Auth0 and SAP are
integrating A2A support into their platforms and agents.]{.c7}

[As an open-source protocol, A2A welcomes community contributions to
facilitate its evolution and widespread adoption.]{.c7}

## [Core Concepts of A2A]{.c5} {#Chapter15InterAgentCommunication(A2A).xhtml_h.cd37wm3k2zft .c9}

[The A2A protocol provides a structured approach for agent interactions,
built upon several core concepts.]{.c7} [ ]{.c7} [A ]{.c7}
[thorough]{.c3} [ grasp of these concepts is crucial for anyone
developing or integrating with A2A-compliant systems.]{.c7} [ ]{.c7}
[The foundational pillars of A2A include Core Actors, Agent Card, Agent
Discovery, Communication and Tasks,  Interaction mechanisms, and
Sec]{.c7} [urity, ]{.c3} [all of which will be reviewed in detail.]{.c7}

[Core Actors:]{.c11} [ A2A involves three main entities:]{.c12}

- [User: Initiates requests for agent assistance.]{.c12}
- [A2A Client (Client Agent): An application or AI agent that acts on
  the user\'s behalf to request actions or information.]{.c12}
- [A2A Server (Remote Agent): An AI agent or system that provides an
  HTTP endpoint to process client requests and return results.]{.c12}
  [ ]{.c12} [The remote agent operates as an \"opaque\" system, meaning
  the client does not need to understand its internal operational
  details.]{.c12}

[Agent Card:]{.c11} [ ]{.c12} [An agent\'s digital identity is defined
by its Agent Card, usually a JSON file.]{.c15} [ ]{.c15} [This file
contains key information for client interaction and automatic discovery,
including the agent\'s identity, endpoint URL, and version.]{.c15}
[ ]{.c15} [It also details supported capabilities like streaming or push
notifications, specific skills, default input/output modes, and
authentication requirements.]{.c15} [ ]{.c15} [Below is an example of an
Agent Card for a WeatherBot.]{.c15}

[]{.c7}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [{]{.c21}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"name\": \"WeatherBot\",]{.c21}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"description\": \"Provides accurate weather forecasts and          |
| historical data.\",]{.c21}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"url\": \"http://weather-service.example.com/a2a\",]{.c21}         |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"version\": \"1.0.0\",]{.c21}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"capabilities\": {]{.c21}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"streaming\": true,]{.c21}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"pushNotifications\": false,]{.c21}                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"stateTransitionHistory\": true]{.c21}                           |
|                                                                       |
| [ },]{.c21}                                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"authentication\": {]{.c21}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"schemes\": \[]{.c21}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"apiKey\"]{.c21}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [   \]]{.c21}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [ },]{.c21}                                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"defaultInputModes\": \[]{.c21}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"text\"]{.c21}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [ \],]{.c21}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"defaultOutputModes\": \[]{.c21}                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"text\"]{.c21}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [ \],]{.c21}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"skills\": \[]{.c21}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [   {]{.c21}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"id\": \"get_current_weather\",]{.c21}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"name\": \"Get Current Weather\",]{.c21}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"description\": \"Retrieve real-time weather for any           |
| location.\",]{.c21}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"inputModes\": \[]{.c21}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"text\"]{.c21}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [     \],]{.c21}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"outputModes\": \[]{.c21}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"text\"]{.c21}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [     \],]{.c21}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"examples\": \[]{.c21}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"What\'s the weather in Paris?\",]{.c21}                     |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"Current conditions in Tokyo\"]{.c21}                        |
|                                                                       |
| [     \],]{.c21}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"tags\": \[]{.c21}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"weather\",]{.c21}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"current\",]{.c21}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"real-time\"]{.c21}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [     \]]{.c21}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   },]{.c21}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   {]{.c21}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"id\": \"get_forecast\",]{.c21}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"name\": \"Get Forecast\",]{.c21}                              |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"description\": \"Get 5-day weather predictions.\",]{.c21}     |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"inputModes\": \[]{.c21}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"text\"]{.c21}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [     \],]{.c21}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"outputModes\": \[]{.c21}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"text\"]{.c21}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [     \],]{.c21}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"examples\": \[]{.c21}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"5-day forecast for New York\",]{.c21}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"Will it rain in London this weekend?\"]{.c21}               |
|                                                                       |
| [     \],]{.c21}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"tags\": \[]{.c21}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"weather\",]{.c21}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"forecast\",]{.c21}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"prediction\"]{.c21}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [     \]]{.c21}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   }]{.c21}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [ \]]{.c21}                                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [}]{.c21}                                                             |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c7}

[Agent discovery:]{.c22} [ it allows clients to find Agent Cards, which
describe the capabilities of available A2A Servers.]{.c7} [ ]{.c7}
[Several strategies exist for this process:]{.c7}

- [Well-Known URI:]{.c23} [ Agents host their Agent Card at a
  standardized path (e.g., ]{.c24} [/.well-known/agent.json).]{.c24}
  [ ]{.c24} [This approach offers broad, often automated, accessibility
  for public or domain-specific use.]{.c24}
- [Curated Registries]{.c23} [:]{.c25} [ These provide a centralized
  catalog where Agent Cards are published and can be queried based on
  specific criteria.]{.c24} [ ]{.c24} [This is well-suited for
  enterprise environments needing centralized management and access
  control.]{.c24}
- [Direct Configuration]{.c23} [:]{.c25} [ Agent Card information is
  embedded or privately shared.]{.c24} [ ]{.c24} [This method is
  appropriate for closely coupled or private systems where dynamic
  discovery isn\'t crucial.]{.c24}

[Regardless of the chosen method, it is important to secure Agent Card
endpoints.]{.c3} [ ]{.c3} [This can be achieved through access control,
mutual TLS (mTLS), or network restrictions, especially if the card
contains sensitive (though non-secret) information.]{.c3}

[Communications and Tasks:]{.c26} [ ]{.c3} [In the A2A framework,
communication is structured around asynchronous tasks, which represent
the fundamental units of work for long-running processes. Each task is
assigned a unique identifier and moves through a series of states---such
as submitted, working, or completed---a design that supports parallel
processing in complex operations. Communication between agents occurs
through a Message. ]{.c7}

[This communication ]{.c3} [ contains a]{.c3} [ttributes,]{.c3} [ which
are key-value metadata describing the message (like its priority or
creation time), and one or more p]{.c3} [arts]{.c3} [, which carry the
actual content being delivered, such as plain text, files, or structured
JSON data. The tangible outputs generated by an agent during a task are
called a]{.c3} [rtifacts.]{.c3} [ Like messages, artifacts are also
composed of one or more parts and can be streamed incrementally as
results become available. All communication within the A2A framework is
conducted over HTTP(S) using the JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol for payloads. To
maintain continuity across multiple interactions, a server-generated
contextId is used to group related tasks and preserve context.]{.c7}

[Interaction Mechanisms]{.c26} [: ]{.c3} [Request/Response (Polling)
]{.c3} [Server-Sent Events (SSE)]{.c3} [.]{.c3} [ A2A provides multiple
interaction methods to suit a variety of AI application needs, each with
a distinct mechanism:]{.c7}

- [Synchronous Request/Response: For quick, immediate operations. In
  this model, the client sends a request and actively waits for the
  server to process it and return a complete response in a single,
  synchronous exchange.]{.c7}
- [Asynchronous Polling: Suited for tasks that take longer to process.
  The client sends a request, and the server immediately acknowledges it
  with a \"working\" status and a task ID. The client is then free to
  perform other actions and can periodically poll the server by sending
  new requests to check the status of the task until it is marked as
  \"completed\" or \"failed.\"]{.c7}
- [Streaming Updates (Server-Sent Events - SSE): Ideal for receiving
  real-time, incremental results. This method establishes a persistent,
  one-way connection from the server to the client. It allows the remote
  agent to continuously push updates, such as status changes or partial
  results, without the client needing to make multiple requests.]{.c7}
- [Push Notifications (Webhooks): Designed for very long-running or
  resource-intensive tasks where maintaining a constant connection or
  frequent polling is inefficient. The client can register a webhook
  URL, and the server will send an asynchronous notification (a
  \"push\") to that URL when the task\'s status changes significantly
  (e.g., upon completion).]{.c7}

[The Agent Card specifies whether an agent supports streaming or push
notification capabilities. Furthermore, A2A is modality-agnostic,
meaning it can facilitate these interaction patterns not just for text,
but also for other data types like audio and video, enabling rich,
multimodal AI applications. ]{.c3} [Both streaming and push notification
capabilities are specified within the Agent Card.]{.c3}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [#Synchronous Request Example]{.c21}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [{]{.c21}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\",]{.c21}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"id\": \"1\",]{.c21}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"method\": \"sendTask\",]{.c21}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"params\": {]{.c21}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"id\": \"task-001\",]{.c21}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"sessionId\": \"session-001\",]{.c21}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"message\": {]{.c21}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"role\": \"user\",]{.c21}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"parts\": \[]{.c21}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [       {]{.c21}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [         \"type\": \"text\",]{.c21}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [         \"text\": \"What is the exchange rate from USD to           |
| EUR?\"]{.c21}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [       }]{.c21}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [     \]]{.c21}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   },]{.c21}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"acceptedOutputModes\": \[\"text/plain\"\],]{.c21}               |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"historyLength\": 5]{.c21}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [ }]{.c21}                                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [}]{.c21}                                                             |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c7}

[T]{.c3} [he ]{.c3} [synchronous request uses the sendTask method, where
the client asks for and expects a single, complete answer to its query.
In contrast, the streaming request uses the sendTaskSubscribe method to
establish a persistent connection, allowing the agent to send back
multiple, incremental updates or partial results over time.]{.c7}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [\# Streaming Request Example]{.c21}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [{]{.c21}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\",]{.c21}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"id\": \"2\",]{.c21}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"method\": \"sendTaskSubscribe\",]{.c21}                           |
|                                                                       |
| [ \"params\": {]{.c21}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"id\": \"task-002\",]{.c21}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"sessionId\": \"session-001\",]{.c21}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"message\": {]{.c21}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"role\": \"user\",]{.c21}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [     \"parts\": \[]{.c21}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [       {]{.c21}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [         \"type\": \"text\",]{.c21}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [         \"text\": \"What\'s the exchange rate for JPY to GBP        |
| today?\"]{.c21}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [       }]{.c21}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [     \]]{.c21}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   },]{.c21}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"acceptedOutputModes\": \[\"text/plain\"\],]{.c21}               |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"historyLength\": 5]{.c21}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [ }]{.c21}                                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [}]{.c21}                                                             |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c7}

[Security:]{.c26} [  ]{.c3} [Inter-Agent Communication (A2A)]{.c3} [:
]{.c3} [Inter-Agent Communication (A2A) is a vital component of system
architecture, enabling secure and seamless data exchange among
agents.]{.c3} [ ]{.c3} [It ensures robustness and integrity through
several built-in mechanisms.]{.c3}

[Mutual Transport Layer Security (TLS): Encrypted and authenticated
connections are established to prevent unauthorized access and data
interception, ensuring secure communication.]{.c3} [ ]{.c7}

[Comprehensive Audit Logs: All inter-agent communications are
meticulously recorded, detailing information flow, involved agents, and
actions.]{.c3} [ ]{.c3} [This audit trail is crucial for accountability,
troubleshooting, and security analysis.]{.c3}

[Agent Card Declaration: Authentication requirements are explicitly
declared in the Agent Card, a configuration artifact outlining the
agent\'s identity, capabilities, and security policies.]{.c3} [ ]{.c3}
[This centralizes and simplifies authentication management.]{.c3}

[Credential Handling: Agents typically authenticate using secure
credentials like OAuth 2.0 tokens or API keys, passed via HTTP
headers.]{.c3} [ ]{.c3} [This method prevents credential exposure in
URLs or message bodies, enhancing overall security.]{.c3}

## [A2A vs. MCP]{.calibre} {#Chapter15InterAgentCommunication(A2A).xhtml_h.i391d9nexxo6 .c9}

[A2A is a protocol that complements Anthropic\'s Model Context Protocol
(MCP) (see Fig. 1).]{.c7} [ ]{.c7} [While MCP focuses on structuring
context for agents and their interaction with external data and tools,
A2A facilitates coordination and communication among agents, enabling
task delegation and collaboration.]{.c7}

[ ![](21/GoogleDoc/images/image2.png){.calibre2} ]{.calibre1}

[Fig.1: Comparison A2A and MCP Protocols]{.c7}

[The goal of A2A is to enhance efficiency, reduce integration costs, and
foster innovation and interoperability in the development of complex,
multi-agent AI systems.]{.c3} [ ]{.c3} [Therefore, a thorough
understanding of A2A\'s core components and operational methods is
essential for its effective design, implementation, and application in
building collaborative and interoperable AI agent systems.]{.c3}
[.]{.c3}

# [Practical Applications & Use Cases]{.c5} {#Chapter15InterAgentCommunication(A2A).xhtml_h.ezipal85wqt2 .c4}

[Inter-Agent Communication is indispensable for building sophisticated
AI solutions across diverse domains, enabling modularity, scalability,
and enhanced intelligence.]{.c7}

- [Multi-Framework Collaboration:]{.c33} [ A2A\'s primary use case is
  enabling independent AI agents, regardless of their underlying
  frameworks (e.g., ADK, LangChain, CrewAI), to communicate and
  collaborate. This is fundamental for building complex multi-agent
  systems where different agents specialize in different aspects of a
  problem.]{.c7}
- [Automated Workflow Orchestration:]{.c33} [ In enterprise settings,
  A2A can facilitate complex workflows by enabling agents to delegate
  and coordinate tasks. For instance, an agent might handle initial data
  collection, then delegate to another agent for analysis, and finally
  to a third for report generation, all communicating via the A2A
  protocol.]{.c7}
- [Dynamic Information Retrieval:]{.c33} [ Agents can communicate to
  retrieve and exchange real-time information. A primary agent might
  request live market data from a specialized \"data fetching agent,\"
  which then uses external APIs to gather the information and send it
  back.]{.c7}

[]{.c7}

# [Hands-On Code Example]{.c5} {#Chapter15InterAgentCommunication(A2A).xhtml_h.4xfposj98oke .c36}

[Let\'s examine the practical applications of the A2A protocol.]{.c3}
[ ]{.c3} [The repository at]{.c3} [
[ ](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://github.com/google-a2a/a2a-samples/tree/main/samples&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228572681033&usg=AOvVaw0nHIsGPINiVGLf-DMlbk-a){.c38}
]{.c3} [
[https://github.com/google-a2a/a2a-samples/tree/main/samples](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://github.com/google-a2a/a2a-samples/tree/main/samples&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228572681375&usg=AOvVaw1ntkcJ3u65SC2l6_hTYeDc){.c38}
]{.c39} [ provides examples in Java, Go, and Python that illustrate how
various agent frameworks, such as LangGraph, CrewAI, Azure AI Foundry,
and AG2, can communicate using A2A. All code in this repository is
released under the Apache 2.0 license.]{.c12} [ ]{.c12} [To further
illustrate A2A\'s core concepts, we will review code excerpts focusing
on setting up an]{.c12} [ A2A Server using an ADK-based agent with
Google-authenticated tools]{.c12} [.]{.c12} [ Looking at ]{.c3} [
[https://github.com/google-a2a/a2a-samples/blob/main/samples/python/agents/birthday_planner_adk/calendar_agent/adk_agent.py](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://github.com/google-a2a/a2a-samples/blob/main/samples/python/agents/birthday_planner_adk/calendar_agent/adk_agent.py&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228572682887&usg=AOvVaw1CYic4W8ZORJzJTkq3Ky2a){.c38}
]{.c39} [ ]{.c7}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [import datetime]{.c21}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.agents import LlmAgent \# type:                      |
| ignore\[import-untyped\]]{.c21}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.tools.google_api_tool import CalendarToolset \#      |
| type: ignore\[import-untyped\]]{.c21}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c21}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [async def create_agent(client_id, client_secret) -\>                 |
| LlmAgent:]{.c21}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"Constructs the ADK agent.\"\"\"]{.c21}                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   toolset = CalendarToolset(client_id=client_id,                    |
| client_secret=client_secret)]{.c21}                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [   return LlmAgent(]{.c21}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [       model=\'gemini-2.0-flash-001\',]{.c21}                        |
|                                                                       |
| [       name=\'calendar_agent\',]{.c21}                               |
|                                                                       |
| [       description=\"An agent that can help manage a user\'s         |
| calendar\",]{.c21}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [       instruction=f\"\"\"]{.c21}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [You are an agent that can help manage a user\'s calendar.]{.c21}     |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c21}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [Users will request information about the state of their calendar     |
| ]{.c21}                                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [or to make changes to their calendar. Use the provided tools for     |
| interacting with the calendar API.]{.c21}                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c21}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [If not specified, assume the calendar the user wants is the          |
| \'primary\' calendar.]{.c21}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c21}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [When using the Calendar API tools, use well-formed RFC3339           |
| timestamps.]{.c21}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c21}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [Today is {datetime.datetime.now()}.]{.c21}                           |
|                                                                       |
| [\"\"\",]{.c21}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [       tools=await toolset.get_tools(),]{.c21}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   )]{.c21}                                                          |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c7}

[This Python code defines an asynchronous function \`create_agent\` that
constructs an ADK LlmAgent.]{.c3} [ ]{.c3} [It begins by initializing a
\`CalendarToolset\` using ]{.c3} [the ]{.c3} [provided client
credentials to access the Google Calendar API. Subsequently, an
\`LlmAgent\` instance is created, configured with a specified Gemini
model, a descriptive name, and instructions for managing a user\'s
calendar.]{.c3} [ ]{.c3} [The agent is furnished with calendar tools
from the \`CalendarToolset\`, enabling it to interact with the Calendar
API and respond to user queries regarding calendar states or
modifications.]{.c3} [ ]{.c3} [The agent\'s instructions dynamically
incorporate the current date for temporal context.]{.c3} [ To illustrate
how an agent is constructed, let\'s examine a key section from the
calendar_agent found in the A2A samples on GitHub. ]{.c7}

[The code below shows how the agent is defined with its specific
instructions and tools. Please note that only the code required to
explain this functionality is shown; you can access the complete file
here:]{.c3} [
[ ](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://github.com/google-a2a/a2a-samples/blob/main/samples/python/agents/birthday_planner_adk/calendar_agent/__main__.py&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228572698009&usg=AOvVaw3pS8wZdPfBooMPyD9EAlks){.c38}
]{.c3} [
[https://github.com/a2aproject/a2a-samples/blob/main/samples/python/agents/birthday_planner_adk/calendar_agent/\_\_main\_\_.py](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://github.com/a2aproject/a2a-samples/blob/main/samples/python/agents/birthday_planner_adk/calendar_agent/__main__.py&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228572698593&usg=AOvVaw356vKSi-q6xU7mnsl0P5wD){.c38}
]{.c39} [ ]{.c7}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [def main(host: str, port: int):]{.c21}                               |
|                                                                       |
| [   # Verify an API key is set.]{.c21}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [   # Not required if using Vertex AI APIs.]{.c21}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [   if os.getenv(\'GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI\') != \'TRUE\' and not   |
| os.getenv(]{.c21}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [       \'GOOGLE_API_KEY\']{.c21}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   ):]{.c21}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [       raise ValueError(]{.c21}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [           \'GOOGLE_API_KEY environment variable not set and         |
| \']{.c21}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [           \'GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI is not TRUE.\']{.c21}         |
|                                                                       |
| [       )]{.c21}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c21}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   skill = AgentSkill(]{.c21}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [       id=\'check_availability\',]{.c21}                             |
|                                                                       |
| [       name=\'Check Availability\',]{.c21}                           |
|                                                                       |
| [       description=\"Checks a user\'s availability for a time using  |
| their Google Calendar\",]{.c21}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [       tags=\[\'calendar\'\],]{.c21}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [       examples=\[\'Am I free from 10am to 11am                      |
| tomorrow?\'\],]{.c21}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [   )]{.c21}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c21}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   agent_card = AgentCard(]{.c21}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [       name=\'Calendar Agent\',]{.c21}                               |
|                                                                       |
| [       description=\"An agent that can manage a user\'s              |
| calendar\",]{.c21}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [       url=f\'http://{host}:{port}/\',]{.c21}                        |
|                                                                       |
| [       version=\'1.0.0\',]{.c21}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [       defaultInputModes=\[\'text\'\],]{.c21}                        |
|                                                                       |
| [       defaultOutputModes=\[\'text\'\],]{.c21}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [       capabilities=AgentCapabilities(streaming=True),]{.c21}        |
|                                                                       |
| [       skills=\[skill\],]{.c21}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   )]{.c21}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c21}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   adk_agent = asyncio.run(create_agent(]{.c21}                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       client_id=os.getenv(\'GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID\'),]{.c21}             |
|                                                                       |
| [       client_secret=os.getenv(\'GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET\'),]{.c21}     |
|                                                                       |
| [   ))]{.c21}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   runner = Runner(]{.c21}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [       app_name=agent_card.name,]{.c21}                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       agent=adk_agent,]{.c21}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [       artifact_service=InMemoryArtifactService(),]{.c21}            |
|                                                                       |
| [       session_service=InMemorySessionService(),]{.c21}              |
|                                                                       |
| [       memory_service=InMemoryMemoryService(),]{.c21}                |
|                                                                       |
| [   )]{.c21}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   agent_executor = ADKAgentExecutor(runner, agent_card)]{.c21}      |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c21}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   async def handle_auth(request: Request) -\>                       |
| PlainTextResponse:]{.c21}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [       await agent_executor.on_auth_callback(]{.c21}                 |
|                                                                       |
| [           str(request.query_params.get(\'state\')),                 |
| str(request.url)]{.c21}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [       )]{.c21}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       return PlainTextResponse(\'Authentication                     |
| successful.\')]{.c21}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c21}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   request_handler = DefaultRequestHandler(]{.c21}                   |
|                                                                       |
| [       agent_executor=agent_executor,                                |
| task_store=InMemoryTaskStore()]{.c21}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [   )]{.c21}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c21}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   a2a_app = A2AStarletteApplication(]{.c21}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [       agent_card=agent_card, http_handler=request_handler]{.c21}    |
|                                                                       |
| [   )]{.c21}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   routes = a2a_app.routes()]{.c21}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [   routes.append(]{.c21}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [       Route(]{.c21}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [           path=\'/authenticate\',]{.c21}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [           methods=\[\'GET\'\],]{.c21}                               |
|                                                                       |
| [           endpoint=handle_auth,]{.c21}                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       )]{.c21}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   )]{.c21}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   app = Starlette(routes=routes)]{.c21}                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c21}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   uvicorn.run(app, host=host, port=port)]{.c21}                     |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c21}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [if \_\_name\_\_ == \'\_\_main\_\_\':]{.c21}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   main()]{.c21}                                                     |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c7}

[This Python code demonstrates setting up an A2A-compliant \"Calendar
Agent\" for checking user availability using Google Calendar.]{.c3}
[ ]{.c3} [It involves verifying API keys or Vertex AI configurations for
authentication purposes.]{.c3} [ ]{.c3} [The agent\'s capabilities,
including the \"check_availability\" skill, are defined within an
AgentCard, which also specifies the agent\'s network address.]{.c3}
[ ]{.c3} [Subsequently, an ADK agent is created, configured with
in-memory services for managing artifacts, sessions, and memory.]{.c3}
[ ]{.c3} [The code then initializes a]{.c3} [ Starlette]{.c3} [ web
application, incorporates an authentication callback and the A2A
protocol handler, and executes it using Uvicorn to expose the agent via
HTTP.]{.c3}

[These examples illustrate the process of building an A2A-compliant
agent, from defining its capabilities to running it as a web
service.]{.c3} [ ]{.c3} [By utilizing Agent Cards and ADK, developers
can create interoperable AI agents capable of integrating with tools
like Google Calendar.]{.c3} [ ]{.c3} [This practical approach
demonstrates the application of A2A in establishing a multi-agent
ecosystem.]{.c3}

[Further exploration of A2A is recommended through the code
demonstration at]{.c3} [
[ ](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://www.trickle.so/blog/how-to-build-google-a2a-project&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228572716337&usg=AOvVaw3YLOUAgFZGPHfFSnojHn78){.c38}
]{.c3} [
[https://www.trickle.so/blog/how-to-build-google-a2a-project](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://www.trickle.so/blog/how-to-build-google-a2a-project&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228572716591&usg=AOvVaw10imGGhIivAISXxASid63U){.c38}
]{.c39} [.]{.c12} [ ]{.c12} [Resources available at this link include
sample A2A clients and servers in Python and JavaScript, multi-agent web
applications, command-line interfaces, and example implementations for
various agent frameworks.]{.c12}

# [At a Glance]{.c5} {#Chapter15InterAgentCommunication(A2A).xhtml_h.u8p7uwpagv32 .c4}

[What: ]{.c11} [Individual AI agents, especially those built on
different frameworks, often struggle with complex, multi-faceted
problems on their own. The primary challenge is the lack of a common
language or protocol that allows them to communicate and collaborate
effectively. This isolation prevents the creation of sophisticated
systems where multiple specialized agents can combine their unique
skills to solve larger tasks. Without a standardized approach,
integrating these disparate agents is costly, time-consuming, and
hinders the development of more powerful, cohesive AI solutions.]{.c15}

[Why: ]{.c11} [The Inter-Agent Communication (A2A) protocol provides an
open, standardized solution for this problem. It is an HTTP-based
protocol that enables interoperability, allowing distinct AI agents to
coordinate, delegate tasks, and share information seamlessly, regardless
of their underlying technology. A core component is the ]{.c12} [Agent
Card]{.c42} [, a digital identity file that describes an agent\'s
capabilities, skills, and communication endpoints, facilitating
discovery and interaction. A2A defines various interaction mechanisms,
including synchronous and asynchronous communication, to support diverse
use cases. By creating a universal standard for agent collaboration, A2A
fosters a modular and scalable ecosystem for building complex,
multi-agent Agentic systems.]{.c15}

[Rule of thumb: ]{.c11} [Use this pattern when you need to orchestrate
collaboration between two or more AI agents, especially if they are
built using different frameworks (e.g., Google ADK, LangGraph, CrewAI).
It is ideal for building complex, modular applications where specialized
agents handle specific parts of a workflow, such as delegating data
analysis to one agent and report generation to another. This pattern is
also essential when an agent needs to dynamically discover and consume
the capabilities of other agents to complete a task.]{.c15}

[Visual summary]{.c43}

[ ![](21/GoogleDoc/images/image1.png){.calibre2} ]{.calibre1}

[Fig.2: A2A inter-agent communication pattern]{.c15}

[]{.c44}

# [Key Takeaways]{.c5} {#Chapter15InterAgentCommunication(A2A).xhtml_h.6xl84av12un7 .c4}

[Key ]{.c3} [Takeaways]{.c3} [:]{.c3}

- [The Google A2A protocol is an open, HTTP-based standard that
  facilitates communication and collaboration between AI agents built
  with different frameworks.]{.c3}
- [An AgentCard serves as a digital identifier for an agent, allowing
  for automatic discovery and understanding of its capabilities by other
  agents.]{.c3}
- [A2A offers both synchronous request-response interactions (using
  \`tasks/send\`) and streaming updates (using \`tasks/sendSubscribe\`)
  to accommodate varying communication needs.]{.c3}
- [The protocol supports multi-turn conversations, including an
  \`input-required\` state, which allows agents to request additional
  information and maintain context during interactions.]{.c3}
- [A2A encourages a modular architecture where specialized agents can
  operate independently on different ports, enabling system scalability
  and distribution.]{.c3}
- [Tools such as Trickle AI aid in visualizing and tracking A2A
  communications, which helps developers monitor, debug, and optimize
  multi-agent systems.]{.c3}
- [While A2A is a high-level protocol for managing tasks and workflows
  between different agents, the Model Context Protocol (MCP) provides a
  standardized interface for LLMs to interface with external
  resources]{.c7}

# [Conclusions]{.c5} {#Chapter15InterAgentCommunication(A2A).xhtml_h.p81y22d69wny .c36}

[The Inter-Agent Communication (A2A) protocol establishes a vital, open
standard to overcome the inherent isolation of individual AI agents. By
providing a common HTTP-based framework, it ensures seamless
collaboration and interoperability between agents built on different
platforms, such as Google ADK, LangGraph, or CrewAI. A core component is
the ]{.c3} [Agent Card]{.c3} [, which serves as a digital identity,
clearly defining an agent\'s capabilities and enabling dynamic discovery
by other agents. The protocol\'s flexibility supports various
interaction patterns, including synchronous requests, asynchronous
polling, and real-time streaming, catering to a wide range of
application needs.]{.c7}

[This enables the creation of modular and scalable architectures where
specialized agents can be combined to orchestrate complex automated
workflows. Security is a fundamental aspect, with built-in mechanisms
like mTLS and explicit authentication requirements to protect
communications. While complementing other standards like MCP, A2A\'s
unique focus is on the high-level coordination and task delegation
between agents. The strong backing from major technology companies and
the availability of practical implementations highlight its growing
importance. This protocol paves the way for developers to build more
sophisticated, distributed, and intelligent multi-agent systems.
Ultimately, A2A is a foundational pillar for fostering an innovative and
interoperable ecosystem of collaborative AI.]{.c7}

[]{.c7}

[]{#Chapter16ResourceAwareOptimization.xhtml}

[Chapter 16: ]{.calibre} [Resource-Aware Optimization]{.calibre}

[Resource-Aware Optimization enables intelligent agents to dynamically
monitor and manage computational, temporal, and financial resources
during operation. This differs from simple planning, which primarily
focuses on action sequencing. Resource-Aware Optimization requires
agents to make decisions regarding action execution to achieve goals
within specified resource budgets or to optimize efficiency. This
involves choosing between more accurate but expensive models and faster,
lower-cost ones, or deciding whether to allocate additional compute for
a more refined response versus returning a quicker, less detailed
answer.]{.c3}

[For example, consider an agent tasked with analyzing a large dataset
for a financial analyst. If the analyst needs a preliminary report
immediately, the agent might use a faster, more affordable model to
quickly summarize key trends. However, if the analyst requires a highly
accurate forecast for a critical investment decision and has a larger
budget and more time, the agent would allocate more resources to utilize
a powerful, slower, but more precise predictive model. A key strategy in
this category is the fallback mechanism, which acts as a safeguard when
a preferred model is unavailable due to being overloaded or throttled.
To ensure graceful degradation, the system automatically switches to a
default or more affordable model, maintaining service continuity instead
of failing completely.]{.c3}

# [Practical ]{.calibre} [Applications & Use Cases]{.c5} {#Chapter16ResourceAwareOptimization.xhtml_h.5yme1hrnglrw .c4}

[Practical use cases include: ]{.c7}

[]{.c7}

- [Cost-Optimized LLM Usage:]{.c11} [ An agent deciding whether to use a
  large, expensive LLM for complex tasks or a smaller, more affordable
  one for simpler queries, based on a budget constraint.]{.c3}
- [Latency-Sensitive ]{.c11} [Operations]{.c11} [:]{.c11} [ In real-time
  systems, an agent ]{.c13} [chooses]{.c14} [ a faster but potentially
  less comprehensive reasoning path to ensure a timely response.]{.c3}
- [Energy Efficiency:]{.c11} [ For agents deployed on edge devices or
  with limited power, optimizing their processing to conserve battery
  life.]{.c3}
- [Fallback for service reliability:]{.c15} [  An agent automatically
  switches to a backup model when the primary choice is unavailable,
  ensuring service continuity and graceful degradation.]{.c3}
- [Data Usage Management:]{.c11} [ An agent opting for summarized data
  retrieval instead of full dataset downloads to save bandwidth or
  storage.]{.c3}
- [Adaptive Task Allocation:]{.c11} [ In multi-agent systems, agents
  ]{.c13} [self-assign]{.c14} [ tasks based on their current
  computational load or available time.]{.c3}

# [Hands-On Code Example]{.calibre} {#Chapter16ResourceAwareOptimization.xhtml_h.fp80qyd39pd8 .c16}

[An intelligent system for answering user questions can assess the
difficulty of each question. For simple queries, it utilizes a
cost-effective language model such as Gemini Flash. For complex
inquiries, a more powerful, but expensive, language model (like Gemini
Pro) is considered. The decision to use the more powerful model also
depends on resource availability, specifically budget and time
constraints. This system dynamically selects appropriate models.]{.c3}

[For example, consider a travel planner built with a hierarchical agent.
The high-level planning, which involves understanding a user\'s complex
request, breaking it down into a multi-step itinerary, and making
logical decisions, would be managed by a sophisticated and more powerful
LLM like Gemini Pro. This is the \"planner\" agent that requires a deep
understanding of context and the ability to reason.]{.c3}

[However, once the plan is established, the individual tasks within that
plan, such as looking up flight prices, checking hotel availability, or
finding restaurant reviews, are essentially simple, repetitive web
queries. These \"tool function calls\" can be executed by a faster and
more affordable model like Gemini Flash. It is easier to visualize why
the affordable model can be used for these straightforward web searches,
while the intricate planning phase requires the greater intelligence of
the more advanced model to ensure a coherent and logical travel
plan.]{.c3}

[Google\'s ADK supports this approach through its multi-agent
architecture, which allows for modular and scalable applications.]{.c14}
[ ]{.c14} [Different agents can handle specialized tasks.]{.c14}
[ ]{.c14} [Model flexibility enables the direct use of various Gemini
models, including both Gemini Pro and Gemini Flash, or integration of
other models through LiteLLM.]{.c14} [ ]{.c14} [The ADK\'s orchestration
capabilities support dynamic, LLM-driven routing for adaptive
behavior.]{.c14} [ ]{.c14} [Built-in evaluation features allow
systematic assessment]{.c14} [ of agent performance, which can be used
for system refinement]{.c14} [ (see the Chapter on Evaluation and
Monitoring).]{.c3}

[Next, two agents with identical setup but utilizing different models
and costs will be defined.]{.c14}

[]{.c3}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [\# Conceptual Python-like structure, not runnable code]{.c23}        |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c23}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.agents import Agent]{.c23}                           |
|                                                                       |
| [\# from google.adk.models.lite_llm import LiteLlm \# If using models |
| not directly supported by ADK\'s default Agent]{.c23}                 |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c23}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Agent using the more expensive Gemini Pro 2.5]{.c23}              |
|                                                                       |
| [gemini_pro_agent = Agent(]{.c23}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"GeminiProAgent\",]{.c23}                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [   model=\"gemini-2.5-pro\", \# Placeholder for actual model name if |
| different]{.c23}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   description=\"A highly capable agent for complex                  |
| queries.\",]{.c23}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [   instruction=\"You are an expert assistant for complex             |
| problem-solving.\"]{.c23}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c23}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c23}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Agent using the less expensive Gemini Flash 2.5]{.c23}            |
|                                                                       |
| [gemini_flash_agent = Agent(]{.c23}                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [   name=\"GeminiFlashAgent\",]{.c23}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [   model=\"gemini-2.5-flash\", \# Placeholder for actual model name  |
| if different]{.c23}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [   description=\"A fast and efficient agent for simple               |
| queries.\",]{.c23}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [   instruction=\"You are a quick assistant for straightforward       |
| questions.\"]{.c23}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c23}                                                             |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c3}

[A Router Agent can direct queries based on simple metrics like query
length, where shorter queries go to less expensive models and longer
queries to more capable models.]{.c14} [ ]{.c14} [However, a more
sophisticated Router Agent can ]{.c14} [utilize either  LLM ]{.c14} [or
ML models to analyze query nuances and complexity.]{.c14} [ ]{.c14}
[This LLM router can determine which downstream language model is most
suitable.]{.c14} [ ]{.c14} [For example, a query requesting a factual
recall is routed to a flash model, while a complex query requiring deep
analysis is routed to a pro model.]{.c14}

[Optimization techniques can further enhance the LLM router\'s
effectiveness.]{.c14} [ ]{.c14} [Prompt tuning involves crafting prompts
to guide the router LLM for better routing decisions.]{.c14} [ ]{.c14}
[Fine-tuning the LLM router on a dataset of queries and their optimal
model choices improves its accuracy and efficiency.]{.c14} [ ]{.c14}
[This dynamic routing capability balances response quality with
cost-effectiveness.]{.c14}

[]{.c3}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [\# Conceptual Python-like structure, not runnable code]{.c23}        |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c23}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.agents import Agent, BaseAgent]{.c23}                |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.events import Event]{.c23}                           |
|                                                                       |
| [from google.adk.agents.invocation_context import                     |
| InvocationContext]{.c23}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [import asyncio]{.c23}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c23}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [class QueryRouterAgent(BaseAgent):]{.c23}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   name: str = \"QueryRouter\"]{.c23}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [   description: str = \"Routes user queries to the appropriate LLM   |
| agent based on complexity.\"]{.c23}                                   |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c23}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   async def \_run_async_impl(self, context: InvocationContext) -\>  |
| AsyncGenerator\[Event, None\]:]{.c23}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [       user_query = context.current_message.text \# Assuming text    |
| input]{.c23}                                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [       query_length = len(user_query.split()) \# Simple metric:      |
| number of words]{.c23}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c23}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       if query_length \< 20: \# Example threshold for simplicity    |
| vs. complexity]{.c23}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [           print(f\"Routing to Gemini Flash Agent for short query    |
| (length: {query_length})\")]{.c23}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [           # In a real ADK setup, you would \'transfer_to_agent\' or |
| directly invoke]{.c23}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [           # For demonstration, we\'ll simulate a call and yield its |
| response]{.c23}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [           response = await                                          |
| gemini_flash_agent.run_async(context.current_message)]{.c23}          |
|                                                                       |
| [           yield Event(author=self.name, content=f\"Flash Agent      |
| processed: {response}\")]{.c23}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [       else:]{.c23}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [           print(f\"Routing to Gemini Pro Agent for long query       |
| (length: {query_length})\")]{.c23}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [           response = await                                          |
| gemini_pro_agent.run_async(context.current_message)]{.c23}            |
|                                                                       |
| [           yield Event(author=self.name, content=f\"Pro Agent        |
| processed: {response}\")]{.c23}                                       |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c3}

[The Critique Agent evaluates responses from language models]{.c14}
[,]{.c14} [ providing feedback that serves several functions.]{.c14}
[ ]{.c14} [For self-correction, it identifies errors or inconsistencies,
prompting the answering agent to refine its output for improved
quality.]{.c14} [ ]{.c14} [It also systematically assesses responses for
performance monitoring, tracking metrics like accuracy and relevance,
which are used for optimization.]{.c14} [ ]{.c3}

[Additionally, its feedback can signal reinforcement learning or
fine-tuning; consistent identification of inadequate Flash model
responses, for instance, can refine the router agent\'s logic.]{.c14}
[ ]{.c14} [While not directly managing the budget, the Critique Agent
contributes to indirect budget management by identifying suboptimal
routing choices, such as directing simple queries to a Pro model or
complex queries to a Flash model, which leads to poor results.]{.c14}
[ ]{.c14} [This informs adjustments that improve resource allocation and
cost savings.]{.c14} [ ]{.c3}

[The Critique Agent can be configured to review either only the
generated text from the answering agent or both the original query and
the generated text, enabling a comprehensive evaluation of the
response\'s alignment with the initial question.]{.c14}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [CRITIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT = \"\"\"]{.c23}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [You are the \*\*Critic Agent\*\*, serving as the quality assurance   |
| arm of our collaborative research assistant system. Your primary      |
| function is to \*\*meticulously review and challenge\*\* information  |
| from the Researcher Agent, guaranteeing \*\*accuracy, completeness,   |
| and unbiased presentation\*\*.]{.c23}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [Your duties encompass:]{.c23}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [\* \*\*Assessing research findings\*\* for factual correctness,      |
| thoroughness, and potential leanings.]{.c23}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [\* \*\*Identifying any missing data\*\* or inconsistencies in        |
| reasoning.]{.c23}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [\* \*\*Raising critical questions\*\* that could refine or expand    |
| the current understanding.]{.c23}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [\* \*\*Offering constructive suggestions\*\* for enhancement or      |
| exploring different angles.]{.c23}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [\* \*\*Validating that the final output is comprehensive\*\* and     |
| balanced.]{.c23}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [All criticism must be constructive. Your goal is to fortify the      |
| research, not invalidate it. Structure your feedback clearly, drawing |
| attention to specific points for revision. Your overarching aim is to |
| ensure the final research product meets the highest possible quality  |
| standards.]{.c23}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [\"\"\"]{.c23}                                                        |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c3}

[The Critic Agent operates based on a predefined system prompt that
outlines its role, responsibilities, and feedback approach.]{.c14}
[ ]{.c14} [A well-designed prompt for this agent must clearly establish
its function as an evaluator.]{.c14} [ ]{.c14} [It should specify the
areas for critical focus and emphasize providing constructive feedback
rather than mere dismissal.]{.c14} [ ]{.c14} [The prompt should also
encourage the identification of both strengths and weaknesses, and it
must guide the agent on how to structure and present its
feedback.]{.c14}

# [Hands-On Code with OpenAI]{.c5} {#Chapter16ResourceAwareOptimization.xhtml_h.f1whi29g26zk .c26}

[This system uses a resource-aware optimization strategy to handle user
queries efficiently. It first classifies each query into one of three
categories to determine the most appropriate and cost-effective
processing pathway. This approach avoids wasting computational resources
on simple requests while ensuring complex queries get the necessary
attention. The three categories are:]{.c3}

- [simple: For straightforward questions that can be answered directly
  without complex reasoning or external data.]{.c3}
- [reasoning: For queries that require logical deduction or multi-step
  thought processes, which are routed to more powerful models.]{.c3}
- [internet_search: For questions needing current information, which
  automatically triggers a Google Search to provide an up-to-date
  answer.]{.c3}

[The code is under the MIT license and available on Github: (]{.c14} [
[https://github.com/mahtabsyed/21-Agentic-Patterns/blob/main/16_Resource_Aware_Opt_LLM_Reflection_v2.ipynb](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://github.com/mahtabsyed/21-Agentic-Patterns/blob/main/16_Resource_Aware_Opt_LLM_Reflection_v2.ipynb&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228577682762&usg=AOvVaw22Xel3yoaVP2cSFl-0Fr09){.c29}
]{.c28} [)]{.c3}

+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [\# MIT License]{.c23}                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| [\# Copyright (c) 2025 Mahtab Syed]{.c23}                                                                                                                                                       |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| [\# ]{.c30} [                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| [https://www.linkedin.com/in/mahtabsyed/](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://www.linkedin.com/in/mahtabsyed/&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228577683903&usg=AOvVaw0ZmAGUIB1yE6vDqqHRhCpU){.c29} |
| ]{.c31}                                                                                                                                                                                         |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| []{.c23}                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| [import os]{.c23}                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| [import requests]{.c23}                                                                                                                                                                         |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| [import json]{.c23}                                                                                                                                                                             |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| [from dotenv import load_dotenv]{.c23}                                                                                                                                                          |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| [from openai import OpenAI]{.c23}                                                                                                                                                               |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| []{.c23}                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| [\# Load environment variables]{.c23}                                                                                                                                                           |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| [load_dotenv()]{.c23}                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| [OPENAI_API_KEY = os.getenv(\"OPENAI_API_KEY\")]{.c23}                                                                                                                                          |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
