    [https://google.github.io/adk-docs/evaluate/](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://google.github.io/adk-docs/evaluate/&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228588284135&usg=AOvVaw0ylgJO_8H3uMK9vAZRrl_z){.c50}
    ]{.c49}
3.  [Survey on Evaluation of LLM-based Agents, ]{.c4} [
    [https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.16416](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.16416&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228588284607&usg=AOvVaw2YO_ovRScb5JV4D7k-RbEc){.c50}
    ]{.c49} [ ]{.c5}
4.  [Agent-as-a-Judge: Evaluate Agents with Agents, ]{.c4} [
    [https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.10934](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.10934&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228588285249&usg=AOvVaw3IpQUopTR4T7pjEnf8-HFT){.c50}
    ]{.c49} [ ]{.c5}
5.  [Agent Companion, gulli et al: ]{.c4} [
    [https://www.kaggle.com/whitepaper-agent-companion](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://www.kaggle.com/whitepaper-agent-companion&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1759228588285942&usg=AOvVaw3EW_h51DXgTqupNI9FLbEl){.c50}
    ]{.c49} [ ]{.c5}

[]{#Chapter20Prioritization.xhtml}

[Chapter 20: Prioritization]{.c2}

[In complex, dynamic environments, Agents frequently encounter numerous
potential actions, conflicting goals, and limited resources.]{.c4}
[ ]{.c4} [Without a defined process for determining the subsequent
action, the agents may experience reduced efficiency, operational
delays, or failures to achieve key objectives.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [The
prioritization pattern addresses this issue by enabling agents to assess
and rank tasks, objectives, or actions based on their significance,
urgency, dependencies, and established criteria.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [This
ensures the agents concentrate efforts on the most critical tasks,
resulting in enhanced effectiveness and goal alignment.]{.c4}

# [Prioritization Pattern Overview]{.c6} {#Chapter20Prioritization.xhtml_h.l5gasv9olxkn .c5}

[Agents employ prioritization to effectively manage tasks, goals, and
sub-goals, guiding subsequent actions.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [This process
facilitates informed decision-making when addressing multiple demands,
prioritizing vital or urgent activities over less critical ones.]{.c4}
[ ]{.c4} [It is particularly relevant in real-world scenarios where
resources are constrained, time is limited, and objectives may
conflict.]{.c4}

[The fundamental aspects of agent prioritization typically involve
several elements.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [First, criteria definition establishes
the rules or metrics for task evaluation.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [These may
include urgency (time sensitivity of the task), importance (impact on
the primary objective), dependencies (whether the task is a prerequisite
for others), resource availability (readiness of necessary tools or
information), cost/benefit analysis (effort versus expected outcome),
and user preferences for personalized agents.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [Second,
task evaluation involves assessing each potential task against these
defined criteria, utilizing methods ranging from simple rules to complex
scoring or reasoning by LLMs.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [Third, scheduling or
selection logic refers to the algorithm that, based on the evaluations,
selects the optimal next action or task sequence, potentially utilizing
a queue or an advanced planning component.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [Finally,
dynamic re-prioritization allows the agent to modify priorities as
circumstances change, such as the emergence of a new critical event or
an approaching deadline, ensuring agent adaptability and
responsiveness.]{.c4}

[Prioritization can occur at various levels: selecting an overarching
objective (high-level goal prioritization), ordering steps within a plan
(sub-task prioritization), or choosing the next immediate action from
available options (action selection).]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [Effective
prioritization enables agents to exhibit more intelligent, efficient,
and robust behavior, especially in complex, multi-objective
environments.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [This mirrors human team organization,
where managers prioritize tasks by considering input from all
members.]{.c4}

# [Practical Applications & Use Cases]{.c6} {#Chapter20Prioritization.xhtml_h.b0qcxu7rrxbi .c5}

[In various real-world applications, AI agents demonstrate a
sophisticated use of prioritization to make timely and effective
decisions. ]{.c8}

- [Automated Customer Support]{.c11} [: Agents prioritize urgent
  requests, like system outage reports, over routine matters, such as
  password resets.]{.c12} [ ]{.c12} [They may also give preferential
  treatment to high-value customers.]{.c12}
- [Cloud Computing]{.c11} [: AI manages and schedules resources by
  prioritizing allocation to critical applications during peak demand,
  while relegating less urgent batch jobs to off-peak hours to optimize
  costs.]{.c12}
- [Autonomous Driving Systems]{.c11} [: Continuously prioritize actions
  to ensure safety and efficiency.]{.c12} [ ]{.c12} [For example,
  braking to avoid a collision takes precedence over maintaining lane
  discipline or optimizing fuel efficiency.]{.c12}
- [Financial Trading]{.c11} [: Bots prioritize trades by analyzing
  factors like market conditions, risk tolerance, profit margins, and
  real-time news, enabling prompt execution of high-priority
  transactions.]{.c12}
- [Project Management]{.c11} [: AI agents prioritize tasks on a project
  board based on deadlines, dependencies, team availability, and
  strategic importance.]{.c12}
- [Cybersecurity]{.c11} [: Agents monitoring network traffic prioritize
  alerts by assessing threat severity, potential impact, and asset
  criticality, ensuring immediate responses to the most dangerous
  threats.]{.c12}
- [Personal Assistant AIs]{.c11} [: Utilize prioritization to manage
  daily lives, organizing calendar events, reminders, and notifications
  according to user-defined importance, upcoming deadlines, and current
  context.]{.c12}

[These examples collectively illustrate how the ability to prioritize is
fundamental to the enhanced performance and decision-making capabilities
of AI agents across a wide spectrum of situations.]{.c4}

# [Hands-On Code Example]{.c6} {#Chapter20Prioritization.xhtml_h.stls7rw0osfi .c5}

[The following demonstrates the development of a Project Manager AI
agent using LangChain.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [This agent facilitates the
creation, prioritization, and assignment of tasks to team members,
illustrating the application of large language models with bespoke tools
for automated project management.]{.c4}

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [import os]{.c17}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [import asyncio]{.c17}                                                |
|                                                                       |
| [from typing import List, Optional, Dict, Type]{.c17}                 |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [from dotenv import load_dotenv]{.c17}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [from pydantic import BaseModel, Field]{.c17}                         |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate]{.c17}         |
|                                                                       |
| [from langchain_core.tools import Tool]{.c17}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI]{.c17}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor,                          |
| create_react_agent]{.c17}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [from langchain.memory import ConversationBufferMemory]{.c17}         |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- 0. Configuration and Setup \-\--]{.c17}                     |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Loads the OPENAI_API_KEY from the .env file.]{.c17}               |
|                                                                       |
| [load_dotenv()]{.c17}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# The ChatOpenAI client automatically picks up the API key from the |
| environment.]{.c17}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [llm = ChatOpenAI(temperature=0.5, model=\"gpt-4o-mini\")]{.c17}      |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- 1. Task Management System \-\--]{.c17}                      |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [class Task(BaseModel):]{.c17}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"Represents a single task in the system.\"\"\"]{.c17}        |
|                                                                       |
| [   id: str]{.c17}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [   description: str]{.c17}                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   priority: Optional\[str\] = None  # P0, P1, P2]{.c17}             |
|                                                                       |
| [   assigned_to: Optional\[str\] = None \# Name of the worker]{.c17}  |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [class SuperSimpleTaskManager:]{.c17}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"An efficient and robust in-memory task                      |
| manager.\"\"\"]{.c17}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [   def \_\_init\_\_(self):]{.c17}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [       # Use a dictionary for O(1) lookups, updates, and             |
| deletions.]{.c17}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [       self.tasks: Dict\[str, Task\] = {}]{.c17}                     |
|                                                                       |
| [       self.next_task_id = 1]{.c17}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   def create_task(self, description: str) -\> Task:]{.c17}          |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"\"\"Creates and stores a new task.\"\"\"]{.c17}             |
|                                                                       |
| [       task_id = f\"TASK-{self.next_task_id:03d}\"]{.c17}            |
|                                                                       |
| [       new_task = Task(id=task_id, description=description)]{.c17}   |
|                                                                       |
| [       self.tasks\[task_id\] = new_task]{.c17}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [       self.next_task_id += 1]{.c17}                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [       print(f\"DEBUG: Task created - {task_id}:                     |
| {description}\")]{.c17}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [       return new_task]{.c17}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   def update_task(self, task_id: str, \*\*kwargs) -\>               |
| Optional\[Task\]:]{.c17}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"\"\"Safely updates a task using Pydantic\'s                 |
| model_copy.\"\"\"]{.c17}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       task = self.tasks.get(task_id)]{.c17}                         |
|                                                                       |
| [       if task:]{.c17}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [           # Use model_copy for type-safe updates.]{.c17}            |
|                                                                       |
| [           update_data = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if v is    |
| not None}]{.c17}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [           updated_task = task.model_copy(update=update_data)]{.c17} |
|                                                                       |
| [           self.tasks\[task_id\] = updated_task]{.c17}               |
|                                                                       |
| [           print(f\"DEBUG: Task {task_id} updated with               |
| {update_data}\")]{.c17}                                               |
|                                                                       |
| [           return updated_task]{.c17}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [          ]{.c17}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [       print(f\"DEBUG: Task {task_id} not found for                  |
| update.\")]{.c17}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [       return None]{.c17}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   def list_all_tasks(self) -\> str:]{.c17}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [       \"\"\"Lists all tasks currently in the system.\"\"\"]{.c17}   |
|                                                                       |
| [       if not self.tasks:]{.c17}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [           return \"No tasks in the system.\"]{.c17}                 |
|                                                                       |
| [      ]{.c17}                                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [       task_strings = \[\]]{.c17}                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [       for task in self.tasks.values():]{.c17}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [           task_strings.append(]{.c17}                               |
|                                                                       |
| [               f\"ID: {task.id}, Desc: \'{task.description}\',       |
| \"]{.c17}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [               f\"Priority: {task.priority or \'N/A\'}, \"]{.c17}    |
|                                                                       |
| [               f\"Assigned To: {task.assigned_to or                  |
| \'N/A\'}\"]{.c17}                                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [           )]{.c17}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [       return \"Current Tasks:\\n\" +                                |
| \"\\n\".join(task_strings)]{.c17}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [task_manager = SuperSimpleTaskManager()]{.c17}                       |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- 2. Tools for the Project Manager Agent \-\--]{.c17}         |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Use Pydantic models for tool arguments for better validation and  |
| clarity.]{.c17}                                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [class CreateTaskArgs(BaseModel):]{.c17}                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   description: str = Field(description=\"A detailed description of  |
| the task.\")]{.c17}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [class PriorityArgs(BaseModel):]{.c17}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [   task_id: str = Field(description=\"The ID of the task to update,  |
| e.g., \'TASK-001\'.\")]{.c17}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   priority: str = Field(description=\"The priority to set. Must be  |
| one of: \'P0\', \'P1\', \'P2\'.\")]{.c17}                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [class AssignWorkerArgs(BaseModel):]{.c17}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   task_id: str = Field(description=\"The ID of the task to update,  |
| e.g., \'TASK-001\'.\")]{.c17}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   worker_name: str = Field(description=\"The name of the worker to  |
| assign the task to.\")]{.c17}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [def create_new_task_tool(description: str) -\> str:]{.c17}           |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"Creates a new project task with the given                   |
| description.\"\"\"]{.c17}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [   task = task_manager.create_task(description)]{.c17}               |
|                                                                       |
| [   return f\"Created task {task.id}:                                 |
| \'{task.description}\'.\"]{.c17}                                      |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [def assign_priority_to_task_tool(task_id: str, priority: str) -\>    |
| str:]{.c17}                                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"Assigns a priority (P0, P1, P2) to a given task             |
| ID.\"\"\"]{.c17}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   if priority not in \[\"P0\", \"P1\", \"P2\"\]:]{.c17}             |
|                                                                       |
| [       return \"Invalid priority. Must be P0, P1, or P2.\"]{.c17}    |
|                                                                       |
| [   task = task_manager.update_task(task_id,                          |
| priority=priority)]{.c17}                                             |
|                                                                       |
| [   return f\"Assigned priority {priority} to task {task.id}.\" if    |
| task else f\"Task {task_id} not found.\"]{.c17}                       |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [def assign_task_to_worker_tool(task_id: str, worker_name: str) -\>   |
| str:]{.c17}                                                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"Assigns a task to a specific worker.\"\"\"]{.c17}           |
|                                                                       |
| [   task = task_manager.update_task(task_id,                          |
| assigned_to=worker_name)]{.c17}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   return f\"Assigned task {task.id} to {worker_name}.\" if task     |
| else f\"Task {task_id} not found.\"]{.c17}                            |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# All tools the PM agent can use]{.c17}                             |
|                                                                       |
| [pm_tools = \[]{.c17}                                                 |
|                                                                       |
| [   Tool(]{.c17}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       name=\"create_new_task\",]{.c17}                              |
|                                                                       |
| [       func=create_new_task_tool,]{.c17}                             |
|                                                                       |
| [       description=\"Use this first to create a new task and get its |
| ID.\",]{.c17}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [       args_schema=CreateTaskArgs]{.c17}                             |
|                                                                       |
| [   ),]{.c17}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   Tool(]{.c17}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       name=\"assign_priority_to_task\",]{.c17}                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       func=assign_priority_to_task_tool,]{.c17}                     |
|                                                                       |
| [       description=\"Use this to assign a priority to a task after   |
| it has been created.\",]{.c17}                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [       args_schema=PriorityArgs]{.c17}                               |
|                                                                       |
| [   ),]{.c17}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   Tool(]{.c17}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       name=\"assign_task_to_worker\",]{.c17}                        |
|                                                                       |
| [       func=assign_task_to_worker_tool,]{.c17}                       |
|                                                                       |
| [       description=\"Use this to assign a task to a specific worker  |
| after it has been created.\",]{.c17}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [       args_schema=AssignWorkerArgs]{.c17}                           |
|                                                                       |
| [   ),]{.c17}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   Tool(]{.c17}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       name=\"list_all_tasks\",]{.c17}                               |
|                                                                       |
| [       func=task_manager.list_all_tasks,]{.c17}                      |
|                                                                       |
| [       description=\"Use this to list all current tasks and their    |
| status.\"]{.c17}                                                      |
|                                                                       |
| [   ),]{.c17}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [\]]{.c17}                                                            |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- 3. Project Manager Agent Definition \-\--]{.c17}            |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [pm_prompt_template = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages(\[]{.c17}      |
|                                                                       |
| [   (\"system\", \"\"\"You are a focused Project Manager LLM agent.   |
| Your goal is to manage project tasks efficiently.]{.c17}              |
|                                                                       |
| [  ]{.c17}                                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   When you receive a new task request, follow these steps:]{.c17}   |
|                                                                       |
| [   1.  First, create the task with the given description using the   |
| \`create_new_task\` tool. You must do this first to get a             |
| \`task_id\`.]{.c17}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [   2.  Next, analyze the user\'s request to see if a priority or an  |
| assignee is mentioned.]{.c17}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [       - If a priority is mentioned (e.g., \"urgent\", \"ASAP\",     |
| \"critical\"), map it to P0. Use \`assign_priority_to_task\`.]{.c17}  |
|                                                                       |
| [       - If a worker is mentioned, use                               |
| \`assign_task_to_worker\`.]{.c17}                                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   3.  If any information (priority, assignee) is missing, you must  |
| make a reasonable default assignment (e.g., assign P1 priority and    |
| assign to \'Worker A\').]{.c17}                                       |
|                                                                       |
| [   4.  Once the task is fully processed, use \`list_all_tasks\` to   |
| show the final state.]{.c17}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [  ]{.c17}                                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   Available workers: \'Worker A\', \'Worker B\', \'Review           |
| Team\']{.c17}                                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [   Priority levels: P0 (highest), P1 (medium), P2 (lowest)]{.c17}    |
|                                                                       |
| [   \"\"\"),]{.c17}                                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [   (\"placeholder\", \"{chat_history}\"),]{.c17}                     |
|                                                                       |
| [   (\"human\", \"{input}\"),]{.c17}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [   (\"placeholder\", \"{agent_scratchpad}\")]{.c17}                  |
|                                                                       |
| [\])]{.c17}                                                           |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Create the agent executor]{.c17}                                  |
|                                                                       |
| [pm_agent = create_react_agent(llm, pm_tools,                         |
| pm_prompt_template)]{.c17}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [pm_agent_executor = AgentExecutor(]{.c17}                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   agent=pm_agent,]{.c17}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   tools=pm_tools,]{.c17}                                            |
|                                                                       |
| [   verbose=True,]{.c17}                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   handle_parsing_errors=True,]{.c17}                                |
|                                                                       |
| [   memory=ConversationBufferMemory(memory_key=\"chat_history\",      |
| return_messages=True)]{.c17}                                          |
|                                                                       |
| [)]{.c17}                                                             |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# \-\-- 4. Simple Interaction Flow \-\--]{.c17}                     |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [async def run_simulation():]{.c17}                                   |
|                                                                       |
| [   print(\"\-\-- Project Manager Simulation \-\--\")]{.c17}          |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   # Scenario 1: Handle a new, urgent feature request]{.c17}         |
|                                                                       |
| [   print(\"\\n\[User Request\] I need a new login system implemented |
| ASAP. It should be assigned to Worker B.\")]{.c17}                    |
|                                                                       |
| [   await pm_agent_executor.ainvoke({\"input\": \"Create a task to    |
| implement a new login system. It\'s urgent and should be assigned to  |
| Worker B.\"})]{.c17}                                                  |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   print(\"\\n\" + \"-\"\*60 + \"\\n\")]{.c17}                       |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   # Scenario 2: Handle a less urgent content update with fewer      |
| details]{.c17}                                                        |
|                                                                       |
| [   print(\"\[User Request\] We need to review the marketing website  |
| content.\")]{.c17}                                                    |
|                                                                       |
| [   await pm_agent_executor.ainvoke({\"input\": \"Manage a new task:  |
| Review marketing website content.\"})]{.c17}                          |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [   print(\"\\n\-\-- Simulation Complete \-\--\")]{.c17}              |
|                                                                       |
| []{.c17}                                                              |
|                                                                       |
| [\# Run the simulation]{.c17}                                         |
|                                                                       |
| [if \_\_name\_\_ == \"\_\_main\_\_\":]{.c17}                          |
|                                                                       |
| [   asyncio.run(run_simulation())]{.c17}                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

[]{.c8}

[T]{.c4} [his]{.c4} [ code implements a simple task management system
using Python and LangChain, designed to simulate a project manager agent
powered by a large language model.]{.c8}

[The system employs a SuperSimpleTaskManager class to efficiently manage
tasks within ]{.c4} [memory]{.c4} [, utilizing a dictionary structure
for rapid data retrieval.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [Each task is represented by a
Task Pydantic model, which encompasses attributes such as a unique
identifier, a descriptive text, an optional priority level (P0, P1, P2),
and an optional assignee designation.]{.c4} [Memory usage varies based
on task type, the number of workers, and other contributing
factors.]{.c8} [ ]{.c4} [The task manager provides methods for task
creation, task modification, and retrieval of all tasks.]{.c4} [ ]{.c8}

[The agent interacts with the task manager via a defined set of
Tools.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [These tools facilitate the creation of new tasks,
the assignment of priorities to tasks, the allocation of tasks to
personnel, and the listing of all tasks.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [Each tool is
encapsulated to enable interaction with an instance of the
SuperSimpleTaskManager.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [Pydantic models are utilized to
delineate the requisite arguments for the tools, thereby ensuring data
validation.]{.c4} [ ]{.c8}

[An AgentExecutor is configured with the language model, the toolset,
and a conversation memory component to maintain contextual
continuity.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [A specific ChatPromptTemplate is defined to
direct the agent\'s behavior in its project management role.]{.c4}
[ ]{.c4} [The prompt instructs the agent to initiate by creating a task,
subsequently assigning priority and personnel as specified, and
concluding with a comprehensive task list.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [Default
assignments, such as P1 priority and \'Worker A\', are stipulated within
the prompt for instances where information is absent.]{.c4} [ ]{.c8}

[The code incorporates a simulation function (run_simulation) of
asynchronous nature to demonstrate the agent\'s operational
capacity.]{.c4} [ ]{.c4} [The simulation executes two distinct
scenarios: the management of an urgent task with designated personnel,
and the management of a less urgent task with minimal input.]{.c4}
[ ]{.c4} [The agent\'s actions and logical processes are outputted to
the console due to the activation of verbose=True within the
AgentExecutor]{.c4} [.]{.c4}

# [At a Glance]{.c6} {#Chapter20Prioritization.xhtml_h.brqtk7a0wium .c5}

[What:]{.c20} [ AI agents operating in complex environments face a
multitude of potential actions, conflicting goals, and finite resources.
Without a clear method to determine their next move, these agents risk
becoming inefficient and ineffective. This can lead to significant
operational delays or a complete failure to accomplish primary
objectives. The core challenge is to manage this overwhelming number of
choices to ensure the agent acts purposefully and logically.]{.c12}

[Why:]{.c20} [ The Prioritization pattern provides a standardized
solution for this problem by enabling agents to rank tasks and goals.
This is achieved by establishing clear criteria such as urgency,
importance, dependencies, and resource cost. The agent then evaluates
each potential action against these criteria to determine the most
critical and timely course of action. This Agentic capability allows the
system to dynamically adapt to changing circumstances and manage
constrained resources effectively. By focusing on the highest-priority
items, the agent\'s behavior becomes more intelligent, robust, and
aligned with its strategic goals.]{.c12}

[Rule of thumb:]{.c20} [ Use the Prioritization pattern when an Agentic
system must autonomously manage multiple, often conflicting, tasks or
goals under resource constraints to operate effectively in a dynamic
environment.]{.c12}

[Visual summary:]{.c20}

[ ![](26/GoogleDoc/images/image1.png){.calibre1} ]{.calibre}

[Fig.1: Prioritization Design pattern]{.c12}

[]{.c2}

# [Key Takeaways]{.c6} {#Chapter20Prioritization.xhtml_h.zak8vvsp7kjg .c5}

- [Prioritization enables AI agents to function effectively in complex,
  multi-faceted environments.]{.c4}
- [Agents utilize established criteria such as urgency, importance, and
  dependencies to evaluate and rank tasks.]{.c4}
- [Dynamic re-prioritization allows agents to adjust their operational
  focus in response to real-time changes.]{.c4} [ ]{.c8}
- [Prioritization occurs at various levels, encompassing overarching
  strategic objectives and immediate tactical decisions.]{.c4} [ ]{.c8}
- [Effective prioritization results in increased efficiency and improved
  operational robustness of AI agents.]{.c4}

# [Conclusions]{.c6} {#Chapter20Prioritization.xhtml_h.vl6ltobt3v1m .c24}

[In conclusion, the prioritization pattern is a cornerstone of effective
agentic AI, equipping systems to navigate the complexities of dynamic
environments with purpose and intelligence. It allows an agent to
autonomously evaluate a multitude of conflicting tasks and goals, making
reasoned decisions about where to focus its limited resources. This
agentic capability moves beyond simple task execution, enabling the
system to act as a proactive, strategic decision-maker. By weighing
criteria such as urgency, importance, and dependencies, the agent
demonstrates a sophisticated, human-like reasoning process.]{.c8}

[A key feature of this agentic behavior is dynamic re-prioritization,
which grants the agent the autonomy to adapt its focus in real-time as
conditions change. As demonstrated in the code example, the agent
interprets ambiguous requests, autonomously selects and uses the
appropriate tools, and logically sequences its actions to fulfill its
objectives. This ability to self-manage its workflow is what separates a
true agentic system from a simple automated script. Ultimately,
mastering prioritization is fundamental for creating robust and
intelligent agents that can operate effectively and reliably in any
complex, real-world scenario.]{.c8}
